authors_text: Sangeet Sankaramangalam Ulhas,Aditya Ravichander,Kathryn A. Johnson,Theodore P. Pavlic,Lance Gharavi,Spring Berman
title: CHARTOPOLIS: A Small-Scale Labor-art-ory for Research and Reflection on Autonomous Vehicles, Human-Robot Interaction, and Sociotechnical Imaginaries
title_zh: CHARTOPOLIS：用于研究和反思自动驾驶车辆、人机交互和社会技术想象的小型劳动艺术
comment: Submission to 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022) Workshop on Miniature Robot Platforms for Full Scale Autonomous Vehicle Research
comments: Submission to 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022) Workshop on Miniature Robot Platforms for Full Scale Autonomous Vehicle Research
abstract: CHARTOPOLIS is a multi-faceted sociotechnical testbed meant to aid in building connections among engineers, psychologists, anthropologists, ethicists, and artists. Superficially, it is an urban autonomous-vehicle testbed that includes both a physical environment for small-scale robotic vehicles as well as a high-fidelity virtual replica that provides extra flexibility by way of computer simulation. However, both environments have been developed to allow for participatory simulation with human drivers as well. Each physical vehicle can be remotely operated by human drivers that have a driver-seat point of view that immerses them within the small-scale testbed, and those same drivers can also pilot high-fidelity models of those vehicles in a virtual replica of the environment. Juxtaposing human driving performance across these two contexts will help identify to what extent human driving behaviors are sensorimotor responses or involve psychological engagement with a system that has physical, not virtual, side effects and consequences. Furthermore, through collaboration with artists, we have designed the physical testbed to make tangible the reality that technological advancement causes the history of a city to fork into multiple, parallel timelines that take place within populations whose increasing isolation effectively creates multiple independent cities in one. Ultimately, CHARTOPOLIS is meant to challenge engineers to take a more holistic view when designing autonomous systems, while also enabling them to gather novel data that will assist them in making these systems more trustworthy.

Ultimately, CHARTOPOLIS is meant to challenge engineers to take a more holistic view when designing autonomous systems, while also enabling them to gather novel data that will assist them in making these systems more trustworthy.
abstract_zh: CHARTOPOLIS 是一个多方面的社会技术测试平台，旨在帮助工程师、心理学家、人类学家、伦理学家和艺术家之间建立联系。从表面上看，它是一个城市自动驾驶车辆测试平台，既包括小型机器人车辆的物理环境，也包括通过计算机模拟提供额外灵活性的高保真虚拟复制品。然而，这两种环境的开发都允许人类驾驶员进行参与式模拟。每辆实体车辆都可以由人类驾驶员远程操作，这些驾驶员具有驾驶员座椅的视角，使他们沉浸在小型测试台中，并且这些驾驶员还可以在环境的虚拟复制品中驾驶这些车辆的高保真模型。将人类驾驶表现在这两种情况下并列，将有助于确定人类驾驶行为在多大程度上属于感觉运动反应，或者涉及与具有物理而非虚拟副作用和后果的系统的心理参与。此外，通过与艺术家的合作，我们设计了物理测试平台，以明确现实：技术进步导致一座城市的历史分裂成多个平行的时间线，这些时间线发生在人口中，而这些人口的日益孤立实际上将多个独立的城市合而为一。最终，CHARTOPOLIS 旨在挑战工程师在设计自主系统时采取更全面的观点，同时也使他们能够收集新颖的数据，帮助他们使这些系统更值得信赖。

AI.tldr: CHARTOPOLIS是一个多维度的社会技术实验平台，旨在通过物理和虚拟环境研究自动驾驶车辆、人机交互及社会技术想象，促进跨学科合作并挑战工程师的系统设计视角。
AI.motivation: 通过结合工程学、心理学、人类学等多学科视角，探索自动驾驶技术对城市历史和社会结构的影响，推动工程师在设计中考虑更广泛的社会技术因素。
AI.method: 构建物理测试场与高保真虚拟环境，允许人类驾驶员在两种场景中远程操作车辆，对比其行为差异；联合艺术家设计具象化城市历史分叉的装置，增强社会技术隐喻。
AI.result: 揭示人类驾驶行为在物理与虚拟环境中的差异，验证技术发展如何导致城市历史的多线程演变，并为自动驾驶系统的信任度提升提供新型数据支持。
AI.conclusion: CHARTOPOLIS证明了跨学科实验平台在技术设计中的必要性，强调工程师需从社会技术整体视角出发，以更包容的方式应对自动驾驶带来的复杂挑战。

search_text: CHARTOPOLIS: A Small-Scale Labor-art-ory for Research and Reflection on Autonomous Vehicles, Human-Robot Interaction, and Sociotechnical Imaginaries CHARTOPOLIS：用于研究和反思自动驾驶车辆、人机交互和社会技术想象的小型劳动艺术 CHARTOPOLIS is a multi-faceted sociotechnical testbed meant to aid in building connections among engineers, psychologists, anthropologists, ethicists, and artists. Superficially, it is an urban autonomous-vehicle testbed that includes both a physical environment for small-scale robotic vehicles as well as a high-fidelity virtual replica that provides extra flexibility by way of computer simulation. However, both environments have been developed to allow for participatory simulation with human drivers as well. Each physical vehicle can be remotely operated by human drivers that have a driver-seat point of view that immerses them within the small-scale testbed, and those same drivers can also pilot high-fidelity models of those vehicles in a virtual replica of the environment. Juxtaposing human driving performance across these two contexts will help identify to what extent human driving behaviors are sensorimotor responses or involve psychological engagement with a system that has physical, not virtual, side effects and consequences. Furthermore, through collaboration with artists, we have designed the physical testbed to make tangible the reality that technological advancement causes the history of a city to fork into multiple, parallel timelines that take place within populations whose increasing isolation effectively creates multiple independent cities in one. Ultimately, CHARTOPOLIS is meant to challenge engineers to take a more holistic view when designing autonomous systems, while also enabling them to gather novel data that will assist them in making these systems more trustworthy. title: A Comparison of Transformer, Convolutional, and Recurrent Neural Networks on Phoneme Recognition
title_zh: Transformer、卷积和循环神经网络在音素识别方面的比较
abstract: Phoneme recognition is a very important part of speech recognition that requires the ability to extract phonetic features from multiple frames. In this paper, we compare and analyze CNN, RNN, Transformer, and Conformer models using phoneme recognition. For CNN, the ContextNet model is used for the experiments. First, we compare the accuracy of various architectures under different constraints, such as the receptive field length, parameter size, and layer depth. Second, we interpret the performance difference of these models, especially when the observable sequence length varies. Our analyses show that Transformer and Conformer models benefit from the long-range accessibility of self-attention through input frames.
summary: Phoneme recognition is a very important part of speech recognition that requires the ability to extract phonetic features from multiple frames. In this paper, we compare and analyze CNN, RNN, Transformer, and Conformer models using phoneme recognition. For CNN, the ContextNet model is used for the experiments. First, we compare the accuracy of various architectures under different constraints, such as the receptive field length, parameter size, and layer depth. Second, we interpret the performance difference of these models, especially when the observable sequence length varies. Our analyses show that Transformer and Conformer models benefit from the long-range accessibility of self-attention through input frames.
abstract_zh: 音素识别是语音识别中非常重要的一部分，需要能够从多个帧中提取语音特征。在本文中，我们比较和分析了使用音素识别的 CNN、RNN、Transformer 和 Conformer 模型。对于CNN，使用ContextNet模型进行实验。首先，我们比较各种架构在不同约束下的准确性，例如感受野长度、参数大小和层深度。其次，我们解释这些模型的性能差异，特别是当可观察序列长度变化时。我们的分析表明，Transformer 和 Conformer 模型受益于通过输入帧进行自我关注的远程可访问性。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了Transformer、卷积神经网络（CNN）、循环神经网络（RNN）及Conformer模型在音素识别任务中的性能。
AI.motivation: 音素识别是语音识别的关键环节，需有效提取多帧语音特征。不同模型在处理序列数据时存在性能差异，需系统比较其优劣。
AI.method: 采用ContextNet实现CNN，对比不同模型在感受野长度、参数量、层数等约束下的准确率，并分析序列长度变化对模型性能的影响。
AI.result: Transformer和Conformer模型因自注意力机制的长距离依赖能力，在处理长序列输入时表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 自注意力机制显著提升了模型对长序列语音特征的建模能力，Transformer及Conformer在音素识别任务中具有优势。

authors_text: Cian Eastwood,Andrei Liviu Nicolicioiu,Julius von Kügelgen,Armin Kekić,Frederik Träuble,Andrea Dittadi,Bernhard Schölkopf
title: DCI-ES: An Extended Disentanglement Framework with Connections to Identifiability
title_zh: DCI-ES：与可识别性相关的扩展解缠结框架
abstract: In representation learning, a common approach is to seek representations which disentangle the underlying factors of variation. Eastwood & Williams (2018) proposed three metrics for quantifying the quality of such disentangled representations: disentanglement (D), completeness (C) and informativeness (I). In this work, we first connect this DCI framework to two common notions of linear and nonlinear identifiability, thereby establishing a formal link between disentanglement and the closely-related field of independent component analysis. We then propose an extended DCI-ES framework with two new measures of representation quality - explicitness (E) and size (S) - and point out how D and C can be computed for black-box predictors. Our main idea is that the functional capacity required to use a representation is an important but thus-far neglected aspect of representation quality, which we quantify using explicitness or ease-of-use (E). We illustrate the relevance of our extensions on the MPI3D and Cars3D datasets.
summary: In representation learning, a common approach is to seek representations which disentangle the underlying factors of variation. Eastwood & Williams (2018) proposed three metrics for quantifying the quality of such disentangled representations: disentanglement (D), completeness (C) and informativeness (I). In this work, we first connect this DCI framework to two common notions of linear and nonlinear identifiability, thereby establishing a formal link between disentanglement and the closely-related field of independent component analysis. We then propose an extended DCI-ES framework with two new measures of representation quality - explicitness (E) and size (S) - and point out how D and C can be computed for black-box predictors. Our main idea is that the functional capacity required to use a representation is an important but thus-far neglected aspect of representation quality, which we quantify using explicitness or ease-of-use (E). We illustrate the relevance of our extensions on the MPI3D and Cars3D datasets.
abstract_zh: 在表征学习中，一种常见的方法是寻找能够解开变化的潜在因素的表征。 Eastwood & Williams (2018) 提出了三个指标来量化这种解开表示的质量：解开性 (D)、完整性 (C) 和信息性 (I)。在这项工作中，我们首先将该 DCI 框架与线性和非线性可辨识性这两个常见概念联系起来，从而在解缠结和密切相关的独立分量分析领域之间建立正式联系。然后，我们提出了一个扩展的 DCI-ES 框架，其中包含两个新的表示质量度量——显性 (E) 和大小 (S)——并指出如何为黑盒预测器计算 D 和 C。我们的主要想法是，使用表示所需的功能能力是表示质量的一个重要但迄今为止被忽视的方面，我们使用显性或易用性（E）对其进行量化。我们说明了 MPI3D 和 Cars3D 数据集上的扩展的相关性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种扩展的DCI-ES框架，用于评估表示学习中解耦表示的质量。
AI.motivation: 在表示学习中，解耦表示的质量评估是一个重要问题，但现有指标未能全面考虑表示的可使用性。
AI.method: 本文将DCI框架与可识别性理论联系起来，并引入了显式性（E）和大小（S）两个新指标，以评估表示的可使用性。
AI.result: 通过在MPI3D和Cars3D数据集上的实验，验证了新指标的有效性，并展示了其对表示质量评估的贡献。
AI.conclusion: 扩展的DCI-ES框架为表示学习中的解耦表示质量评估提供了更全面的分析方法。

search_text: DCI-ES: An Extended Disentanglement Framework with Connections to Identifiability DCI-ES：与可识别性相关的扩展解缠结框架 In representation learning, a common approach is to seek representations which disentangle the underlying factors of variation. Eastwood & Williams (2018) proposed three metrics for quantifying the quality of such disentangled representations: disentanglement (D), completeness (C) and informativeness (I). In this work, we first connect this DCI framework to two common notions of linear and nonlinear identifiability, thereby establishing a formal link between disentanglement and the closely-related field of independent component analysis. We then propose an extended DCI-ES framework with two new measures of representation quality - explicitness (E) and size (S) - and point out how D and C can be computed for black-box predictors. Our main idea is that the functional capacity required to use a representation is an important but thus-far neglected aspect of representation quality, which we quantify using explicitness or ease-of-use (E). We illustrate the relevance of our extensions on the MPI3D and Cars3D datasets. 在表征学习中，一种常见的方法是寻找能够解开变化的潜在因素的表征。 Eastwood & Williams (2018) 提出了三个指标来量化这种解开表示的质量：解开性 (D)、完整性 (C) 和信息性 (I)。在这项工作中，我们首先将该 DCI 框架与线性和非线性可辨识性这两个常见概念联系起来，从而在解缠结和密切相关的独立分量分析领域之间建立正式联系。然后，我们提出了一个扩展的 DCI-ES 框架，其中包含两个新的表示质量度量——显性 (E) 和大小 (S)——并指出如何为黑盒预测器计算 D 和 C。我们的主要想法是，使用表示所需的功能能力是表示质量的一个重要但迄今为止被忽视的方面，我们使用显性或易用性（E）对其进行量化。我们说明了 MPI3D 和 Cars3D 数据集上的扩展的相关性。 本文提出了一种扩展的DCI-ES框架，用于评估表示学习中解耦表示的质量。 Cian Eastwood,Andrei Liviu Nicolicioiu,Julius von Kügelgen,Armin Kekić,Frederik Träuble,Andrea Dittadi,Bernhard Schölkopf cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV,stat.ML

authors_text: Sid Ahmed Fezza,Mohammed Yasser Ouis,Bachir Kaddar,Wassim Hamidouche,Abdenour Hadid
title: Evaluation of Pre-Trained CNN Models for Geographic Fake Image Detection
title_zh: 用于地理假图像检测的预训练 CNN 模型的评估
comment: IEEE International Workshop on Multimedia Signal Processing (MMSP'2022)
comments: IEEE International Workshop on Multimedia Signal Processing (MMSP'2022)
abstract: Thanks to the remarkable advances in generative adversarial networks (GANs), it is becoming increasingly easy to generate/manipulate images. The existing works have mainly focused on deepfake in face images and videos. However, we are currently witnessing the emergence of fake satellite images, which can be misleading or even threatening to national security. Consequently, there is an urgent need to develop detection methods capable of distinguishing between real and fake satellite images. To advance the field, in this paper, we explore the suitability of several convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures for fake satellite image detection. Specifically, we benchmark four CNN models by conducting extensive experiments to evaluate their performance and robustness against various image distortions. This work allows the establishment of new baselines and may be useful for the development of CNN-based methods for fake satellite image detection.
summary: Thanks to the remarkable advances in generative adversarial networks (GANs), it is becoming increasingly easy to generate/manipulate images. The existing works have mainly focused on deepfake in face images and videos. However, we are currently witnessing the emergence of fake satellite images, which can be misleading or even threatening to national security. Consequently, there is an urgent need to develop detection methods capable of distinguishing between real and fake satellite images. To advance the field, in this paper, we explore the suitability of several convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures for fake satellite image detection. Specifically, we benchmark four CNN models by conducting extensive experiments to evaluate their performance and robustness against various image distortions. This work allows the establishment of new baselines and may be useful for the development of CNN-based methods for fake satellite image detection.
abstract_zh: 由于生成对抗网络（GAN）的显着进步，生成/操作图像变得越来越容易。现有的工作主要集中在人脸图像和视频中的深度伪造。然而，我们目前正在目睹虚假卫星图像的出现，这些图像可能会误导甚至威胁国家安全。因此，迫切需要开发能够区分真假卫星图像的检测方法。为了推动这一领域的发展，在本文中，我们探索了几种卷积神经网络（CNN）架构对于虚假卫星图像检测的适用性。具体来说，我们通过进行大量实验来对四种 CNN 模型进行基准测试，以评估它们针对各种图像失真的性能和鲁棒性。这项工作可以建立新的基线，并且可能有助于开发基于 CNN 的虚假卫星图像检测方法。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了多种预训练卷积神经网络（CNN）模型在检测地理虚假图像（如卫星图像）中的适用性。
AI.motivation: 随着生成对抗网络（GANs）的发展，虚假卫星图像可能威胁国家安全，但现有研究主要集中在人脸深伪图像，亟需开发针对卫星图像的检测方法。
AI.method: 通过大量实验对比四种CNN模型在不同图像失真情况下的性能和鲁棒性，建立基准线。
AI.result: 确定了适用于虚假卫星图像检测的CNN模型基线，为后续方法开发提供参考。
AI.conclusion: 本研究为基于CNN的虚假卫星图像检测方法提供了实验依据和改进方向。

authors_text: Zheng Wu,Yichen Xie,Wenzhao Lian,Changhao Wang,Yanjiang Guo,Jianyu Chen,Stefan Schaal,Masayoshi Tomizuka
title: Zero-Shot Policy Transfer with Disentangled Task Representation of Meta-Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 零样本策略转移与元强化学习的解缠结任务表示
abstract: Humans are capable of abstracting various tasks as different combinations of multiple attributes. This perspective of compositionality is vital for human rapid learning and adaption since previous experiences from related tasks can be combined to generalize across novel compositional settings. In this work, we aim to achieve zero-shot policy generalization of Reinforcement Learning (RL) agents by leveraging the task compositionality. Our proposed method is a meta- RL algorithm with disentangled task representation, explicitly encoding different aspects of the tasks. Policy generalization is then performed by inferring unseen compositional task representations via the obtained disentanglement without extra exploration. The evaluation is conducted on three simulated tasks and a challenging real-world robotic insertion task. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves policy generalization to unseen compositional tasks in a zero-shot manner.
summary: Humans are capable of abstracting various tasks as different combinations of multiple attributes. This perspective of compositionality is vital for human rapid learning and adaption since previous experiences from related tasks can be combined to generalize across novel compositional settings. In this work, we aim to achieve zero-shot policy generalization of Reinforcement Learning (RL) agents by leveraging the task compositionality. Our proposed method is a meta- RL algorithm with disentangled task representation, explicitly encoding different aspects of the tasks. Policy generalization is then performed by inferring unseen compositional task representations via the obtained disentanglement without extra exploration. The evaluation is conducted on three simulated tasks and a challenging real-world robotic insertion task. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves policy generalization to unseen compositional tasks in a zero-shot manner.
abstract_zh: 人类能够将各种任务抽象为多个属性的不同组合。这种组合性的视角对于人类的快速学习和适应至关重要，因为可以将相关任务的先前经验结合起来以概括新的组合设置。在这项工作中，我们的目标是通过利用任务组合性来实现强化学习（RL）代理的零样本策略泛化。我们提出的方法是一种元强化学习算法，具有解缠结的任务表示，显式编码任务的不同方面。然后通过所获得的解开来推断未见过的组合任务表示来执行策略泛化，而无需额外的探索。该评估针对三个模拟任务和一个具有挑战性的现实世界机器人插入任务进行。实验结果表明，我们提出的方法以零样本的方式实现了对未见过的组合任务的策略泛化。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过利用任务组合性，实现强化学习代理在零样本情况下的策略泛化。
AI.motivation: 人类能通过组合不同属性抽象任务，使先前经验能泛化到新任务，但现有方法难以在无需额外探索的情况下实现零样本泛化。
AI.method: 提出了一种元强化学习算法，通过解耦任务表示，显式编码任务的不同属性，并通过推断未见过的组合任务表示实现策略泛化，无需额外探索。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在三个模拟任务和一个真实机器人插入任务中成功实现了对未见过的组合任务的零样本策略泛化。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了解耦任务表示在零样本策略迁移中的有效性，为元强化学习提供了新思路。

title: Longitudinal Sentiment Analyses for Radicalization Research: Intertemporal Dynamics on Social Media Platforms and their Implications
title_zh: 激进化研究的纵向情绪分析：社交媒体平台上的跨期动态及其含义
abstract: This discussion paper demonstrates how longitudinal sentiment analyses can depict intertemporal dynamics on social media platforms, what challenges are inherent and how further research could benefit from a longitudinal perspective. Furthermore and since tools for sentiment analyses shall simplify and accelerate the analytical process regarding qualitative data at acceptable inter-rater reliability, their applicability in the context of radicalization research will be examined regarding the Tweets collected on January 6th 2021, the day of the storming of the U.S. Capitol in Washington. Therefore, a total of 49,350 Tweets will be analyzed evenly distributed within three different sequences: before, during and after the U.S. Capitol in Washington was stormed. These sequences highlight the intertemporal dynamics within comments on social media platforms as well as the possible benefits of a longitudinal perspective when using conditional means and conditional variances. Limitations regarding the identification of supporters of such events and associated hate speech as well as common application errors will be demonstrated as well. As a result, only under certain conditions a longitudinal sentiment analysis can increase the accuracy of evidence based predictions in the context of radicalization research.
summary: This discussion paper demonstrates how longitudinal sentiment analyses can depict intertemporal dynamics on social media platforms, what challenges are inherent and how further research could benefit from a longitudinal perspective. Furthermore and since tools for sentiment analyses shall simplify and accelerate the analytical process regarding qualitative data at acceptable inter-rater reliability, their applicability in the context of radicalization research will be examined regarding the Tweets collected on January 6th 2021, the day of the storming of the U.S. Capitol in Washington. Therefore, a total of 49,350 Tweets will be analyzed evenly distributed within three different sequences: before, during and after the U.S. Capitol in Washington was stormed. These sequences highlight the intertemporal dynamics within comments on social media platforms as well as the possible benefits of a longitudinal perspective when using conditional means and conditional variances. Limitations regarding the identification of supporters of such events and associated hate speech as well as common application errors will be demonstrated as well. As a result, only under certain conditions a longitudinal sentiment analysis can increase the accuracy of evidence based predictions in the context of radicalization research.
abstract_zh: 本讨论文件展示了纵向情感分析如何描述社交媒体平台上的跨期动态、固有的挑战以及进一步的研究如何从纵向角度受益。此外，由于情绪分析工具应以可接受的评估者间可靠性简化和加速有关定性数据的分析过程，因此将根据 2021 年 1 月 6 日（华盛顿美国国会大厦遭到袭击的当天）收集的推文来检验它们在激进化研究背景下的适用性。因此，总共 49,350 条推文将被分析，均匀分布在三个不同的序列中：华盛顿美国国会大厦遭到袭击之前、期间和之后。这些序列强调了社交媒体平台评论中的跨期动态，以及使用条件均值和条件方差时纵向视角可能带来的好处。还将展示有关识别此类事件的支持者和相关仇恨言论以及常见应用程序错误的限制。因此，只有在某些条件下，纵向情绪分析才能提高激进化研究背景下基于证据的预测的准确性。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过纵向情感分析研究社交媒体平台上的极端主义动态，以2021年1月6日美国国会大厦被冲击事件的推文为案例。
AI.motivation: 研究旨在展示纵向情感分析如何描绘社交媒体上的时间动态，同时探讨其在极端主义研究中的适用性及挑战。
AI.method: 对2021年1月6日前、中、后三个时间段的49,350条推文进行情感分析，使用条件均值和条件方差来揭示时间动态。
AI.result: 纵向情感分析在某些条件下可提高基于证据的预测准确性，但存在识别支持者和仇恨言论的局限性及常见应用错误。
AI.conclusion: 纵向视角有助于理解社交媒体上的极端主义动态，但需注意其局限性及适用条件。

search_text: Longitudinal Sentiment Analyses for Radicalization Research: Intertemporal Dynamics on Social Media Platforms and their Implications 激进化研究的纵向情绪分析：社交媒体平台上的跨期动态及其含义 This discussion paper demonstrates how longitudinal sentiment analyses can depict intertemporal dynamics on social media platforms, what challenges are inherent and how further research could benefit from a longitudinal perspective. Furthermore and since tools for sentiment analyses shall simplify and accelerate the analytical process regarding qualitative data at acceptable inter-rater reliability, their applicability in the context of radicalization research will be examined regarding the Tweets collected on January 6th 2021, the day of the storming of the U.S. Capitol in Washington. Therefore, a total of 49,350 Tweets will be analyzed evenly distributed within three different sequences: before, during and after the U.S. Capitol in Washington was stormed. These sequences highlight the intertemporal dynamics within comments on social media platforms as well as the possible benefits of a longitudinal perspective when using conditional means and conditional variances. Limitations regarding the identification of supporters of such events and associated hate speech as well as common application errors will be demonstrated as well. As a result, only under certain conditions a longitudinal sentiment analysis can increase the accuracy of evidence based predictions in the context of radicalization research. 本讨论文件展示了纵向情感分析如何描述社交媒体平台上的跨期动态、固有的挑战以及进一步的研究如何从纵向角度受益。此外，由于情绪分析工具应以可接受的评估者间可靠性简化和加速有关定性数据的分析过程，因此将根据 2021 年 1 月 6 日（华盛顿美国国会大厦遭到袭击的当天）收集的推文来检验它们在激进化研究背景下的适用性。因此，总共 49,350 条推文将被分析，均匀分布在三个不同的序列中：华盛顿美国国会大厦遭到袭击之前、期间和之后。这些序列强调了社交媒体平台评论中的跨期动态，以及使用条件均值和条件方差时纵向视角可能带来的好处。还将展示有关识别此类事件的支持者和相关仇恨言论以及常见应用程序错误的限制。因此，只有在某些条件下，纵向情绪分析才能提高激进化研究背景下基于证据的预测的准确性。 该论文通过纵向情感分析研究社交媒体平台上的极端主义动态，以2021年1月6日美国国会大厦被冲击事件的推文为案例。 Dennis Klinkhammer cs.CL,cs.CV,stat.AP

title: Deep Recurrent Q-learning for Energy-constrained Coverage with a Mobile Robot
title_zh: 移动机器人能量受限覆盖的深度循环 Q 学习
abstract: In this paper, we study the problem of coverage of an environment with an energy-constrained robot in the presence of multiple charging stations. As the robot's on-board power supply is limited, it might not have enough energy to cover all the points in the environment with a single charge. Instead, it will need to stop at one or more charging stations to recharge its battery intermittently. The robot cannot violate the energy constraint, i.e., visit a location with negative available energy. To solve this problem, we propose a deep Q-learning framework that produces a policy to maximize the coverage and minimize the budget violations. Our proposed framework also leverages the memory of a recurrent neural network (RNN) to better suit this multi-objective optimization problem. We have tested the presented framework within a 16 x 16 grid environment having charging stations and various obstacle configurations. Results show that our proposed method finds feasible solutions and outperforms a comparable existing technique.
summary: In this paper, we study the problem of coverage of an environment with an energy-constrained robot in the presence of multiple charging stations. As the robot's on-board power supply is limited, it might not have enough energy to cover all the points in the environment with a single charge. Instead, it will need to stop at one or more charging stations to recharge its battery intermittently. The robot cannot violate the energy constraint, i.e., visit a location with negative available energy. To solve this problem, we propose a deep Q-learning framework that produces a policy to maximize the coverage and minimize the budget violations. Our proposed framework also leverages the memory of a recurrent neural network (RNN) to better suit this multi-objective optimization problem. We have tested the presented framework within a 16 x 16 grid environment having charging stations and various obstacle configurations. Results show that our proposed method finds feasible solutions and outperforms a comparable existing technique.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了存在多个充电站的情况下能量受限机器人的环境覆盖问题。由于机器人的机载电源有限，一次充电可能没有足够的能量来覆盖环境中的所有点。相反，它需要停在一个或多个充电站间歇性地为电池充电。机器人不能违反能量约束，即访问可用能量为负的位置。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个深度 Q 学习框架，该框架产生一个最大化覆盖范围并最小化预算违规的策略。我们提出的框架还利用循环神经网络（RNN）的内存来更好地适应这种多目标优化问题。我们在具有充电站和各种障碍物配置的 16 x 16 网格环境中测试了所提出的框架。结果表明，我们提出的方法找到了可行的解决方案，并且优于现有的同类技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度递归Q学习的框架，用于解决能源受限移动机器人在多充电站环境下的覆盖问题。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以在能源约束下同时优化覆盖范围与预算违规，需考虑机器人充电中断的复杂路径规划问题。
AI.method: 结合深度Q学习与循环神经网络（RNN），通过记忆机制处理多目标优化，生成兼顾覆盖最大化与能源约束的策略。
AI.result: 在16x16网格环境中测试表明，该方法能有效找到可行解，并优于现有技术。
AI.conclusion: 所提框架成功解决了能源受限下的覆盖问题，在多充电站场景中表现出更优性能。

title: Using Argumentation Schemes to Model Legal Reasoning
title_zh: 使用论证方案来模拟法律推理
abstract: We present argumentation schemes to model reasoning with legal cases. We provide schemes for each of the three stages that take place after the facts are established: factor ascription, issue resolution and outcome determination. The schemes are illustrated with examples from a specific legal domain, US Trade Secrets law, and the wider applicability of these schemes is discussed.
summary: We present argumentation schemes to model reasoning with legal cases. We provide schemes for each of the three stages that take place after the facts are established: factor ascription, issue resolution and outcome determination. The schemes are illustrated with examples from a specific legal domain, US Trade Secrets law, and the wider applicability of these schemes is discussed.
abstract_zh: 我们提出论证方案来模拟法律案例的推理。我们为事实确定后发生的三个阶段提供方案：因素归属、问题解决和结果确定。这些方案以特定法律领域（美国商业秘密法）的示例进行说明，并讨论了这些方案的更广泛适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用论点方案来建模法律推理，具体针对事实确立后的三个阶段：因素归因、问题解决和结果确定。
AI.motivation: 法律推理需要结构化的方法来处理复杂案例，现有方法在阶段划分和适用性上存在不足。
AI.method: 设计针对三个推理阶段的论点方案，并以美国商业秘密法为例进行实例说明，讨论其广泛适用性。
AI.result: 成功构建了适用于法律案例推理的论点框架，并验证了其在特定法律领域的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为法律推理提供了可复用的模型，具有跨领域应用潜力，未来可扩展至其他法律体系。

title: Learning Hierarchical Image Segmentation For Recognition and By Recognition
title_zh: 学习分层图像分割以进行识别和通过识别
comment: ICLR 2024 (spotlight). First two authors contributed equally. Code available at https://github.com/twke18/CAST
comments: ICLR 2024 (spotlight). First two authors contributed equally. Code available at https://github.com/twke18/CAST
abstract: Large vision and language models learned directly through image-text associations often lack detailed visual substantiation, whereas image segmentation tasks are treated separately from recognition, supervisedly learned without interconnections. Our key observation is that, while an image can be recognized in multiple ways, each has a consistent part-and-whole visual organization. Segmentation thus should be treated not as an end task to be mastered through supervised learning, but as an internal process that evolves with and supports the ultimate goal of recognition. We propose to integrate a hierarchical segmenter into the recognition process, train and adapt the entire model solely on image-level recognition objectives. We learn hierarchical segmentation for free alongside recognition, automatically uncovering part-to-whole relationships that not only underpin but also enhance recognition. Enhancing the Vision Transformer (ViT) with adaptive segment tokens and graph pooling, our model surpasses ViT in unsupervised part-whole discovery, semantic segmentation, image classification, and efficiency. Notably, our model (trained on unlabeled 1M ImageNet images) outperforms SAM (trained on 11M images and 1 billion masks) by absolute 8% in mIoU on PartImageNet object segmentation.
summary: Large vision and language models learned directly through image-text associations often lack detailed visual substantiation, whereas image segmentation tasks are treated separately from recognition, supervisedly learned without interconnections. Our key observation is that, while an image can be recognized in multiple ways, each has a consistent part-and-whole visual organization. Segmentation thus should be treated not as an end task to be mastered through supervised learning, but as an internal process that evolves with and supports the ultimate goal of recognition. We propose to integrate a hierarchical segmenter into the recognition process, train and adapt the entire model solely on image-level recognition objectives. We learn hierarchical segmentation for free alongside recognition, automatically uncovering part-to-whole relationships that not only underpin but also enhance recognition. Enhancing the Vision Transformer (ViT) with adaptive segment tokens and graph pooling, our model surpasses ViT in unsupervised part-whole discovery, semantic segmentation, image classification, and efficiency. Notably, our model (trained on unlabeled 1M ImageNet images) outperforms SAM (trained on 11M images and 1 billion masks) by absolute 8% in mIoU on PartImageNet object segmentation.
abstract_zh: 直接通过图像-文本关联学习的大型视觉和语言模型通常缺乏详细的视觉证实，而图像分割任务与识别分开处理，在没有互连的情况下进行监督学习。我们的主要观察结果是，虽然图像可以通过多种方式识别，但每种方式都具有一致的部分和整体视觉组织。因此，分割不应被视为通过监督学习掌握的最终任务，而应被视为随着识别的最终目标而发展并支持识别的最终目标的内部过程。我们建议将分层分割器集成到识别过程中，仅根据图像级识别目标来训练和调整整个模型。我们在识别的同时免费学习分层分割，自动揭示部分与整体的关系，这不仅支撑而且增强了识别。通过自适应分段标记和图池增强视觉变换器（ViT），我们的模型在无监督部分整体发现、语义分割、图像分类和效率方面超越了 ViT。值得注意的是，我们的模型（在未标记的 100 万张 ImageNet 图像上训练）在 PartImageNet 对象分割的 mIoU 方面比 SAM（在 1100 万张图像和 10 亿个掩模上训练）的性能高出 8%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种将层次化图像分割与识别过程结合的方法，通过无监督学习实现分割与识别的协同优化。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉模型缺乏细粒度视觉依据，分割任务与识别任务分离导致信息割裂，而图像识别存在多种合理分解方式，需建立分割与识别的内在关联。
AI.method: 在Vision Transformer框架中集成层次化分割器，通过自适应分割标记和图池化机制，仅以图像级识别目标进行端到端训练，无需分割标注数据。
AI.result: 在PartImageNet物体分割任务中，使用1M无标签ImageNet图像训练的模型较SAM（使用11M图像+10亿掩码训练）mIoU提升8%，并在语义分割、分类和效率指标上全面超越ViT。
AI.conclusion: 层次化分割应作为识别过程的内在机制而非独立任务，该方法证明了分割与识别的协同训练能提升模型表征能力与任务性能。

search_text: Learning Hierarchical Image Segmentation For Recognition and By Recognition 学习分层图像分割以进行识别和通过识别 Large vision and language models learned directly through image-text associations often lack detailed visual substantiation, whereas image segmentation tasks are treated separately from recognition, supervisedly learned without interconnections. Our key observation is that, while an image can be recognized in multiple ways, each has a consistent part-and-whole visual organization. Segmentation thus should be treated not as an end task to be mastered through supervised learning, but as an internal process that evolves with and supports the ultimate goal of recognition. We propose to integrate a hierarchical segmenter into the recognition process, train and adapt the entire model solely on image-level recognition objectives. We learn hierarchical segmentation for free alongside recognition, automatically uncovering part-to-whole relationships that not only underpin but also enhance recognition. Enhancing the Vision Transformer (ViT) with adaptive segment tokens and graph pooling, our model surpasses ViT in unsupervised part-whole discovery, semantic segmentation, image classification, and efficiency. Notably, our model (trained on unlabeled 1M ImageNet images) outperforms SAM (trained on 11M images and 1 billion masks) by absolute 8% in mIoU on PartImageNet object segmentation. 直接通过图像-文本关联学习的大型视觉和语言模型通常缺乏详细的视觉证实，而图像分割任务与识别分开处理，在没有互连的情况下进行监督学习。我们的主要观察结果是，虽然图像可以通过多种方式识别，但每种方式都具有一致的部分和整体视觉组织。因此，分割不应被视为通过监督学习掌握的最终任务，而应被视为随着识别的最终目标而发展并支持识别的最终目标的内部过程。我们建议将分层分割器集成到识别过程中，仅根据图像级识别目标来训练和调整整个模型。我们在识别的同时免费学习分层分割，自动揭示部分与整体的关系，这不仅支撑而且增强了识别。通过自适应分段标记和图池增强视觉变换器（ViT），我们的模型在无监督部分整体发现、语义分割、图像分类和效率方面超越了 ViT。值得注意的是，我们的模型（在未标记的 100 万张 ImageNet 图像上训练）在 PartImageNet 对象分割的 mIoU 方面比 SAM（在 1100 万张图像和 10 亿个掩模上训练）的性能高出 8%。 本文提出了一种将层次化图像分割与识别过程结合的方法，通过无监督学习实现分割与识别的协同优化。 Tsung-Wei Ke,Sangwoo Mo,Stella X. authors_text: S Ashwin Hebbar,Viraj Nadkarni,Ashok Vardhan Makkuva,Suma Bhat,Sewoong Oh,Pramod Viswanath
title: CRISP: Curriculum based Sequential Neural Decoders for Polar Code Family
title_zh: CRISP：基于课程的 Polar 码系列序列神经解码器
abstract: Polar codes are widely used state-of-the-art codes for reliable communication that have recently been included in the 5th generation wireless standards (5G). However, there remains room for the design of polar decoders that are both efficient and reliable in the short blocklength regime. Motivated by recent successes of data-driven channel decoders, we introduce a novel $\textbf{C}$ur$\textbf{RI}$culum based $\textbf{S}$equential neural decoder for $\textbf{P}$olar codes (CRISP). We design a principled curriculum, guided by information-theoretic insights, to train CRISP and show that it outperforms the successive-cancellation (SC) decoder and attains near-optimal reliability performance on the Polar(32,16) and Polar(64,22) codes. The choice of the proposed curriculum is critical in achieving the accuracy gains of CRISP, as we show by comparing against other curricula. More notably, CRISP can be readily extended to Polarization-Adjusted-Convolutional (PAC) codes, where existing SC decoders are significantly less reliable. To the best of our knowledge, CRISP constructs the first data-driven decoder for PAC codes and attains near-optimal performance on the PAC(32,16) code.
summary: Polar codes are widely used state-of-the-art codes for reliable communication that have recently been included in the 5th generation wireless standards (5G). However, there remains room for the design of polar decoders that are both efficient and reliable in the short blocklength regime. Motivated by recent successes of data-driven channel decoders, we introduce a novel $\textbf{C}$ur$\textbf{RI}$culum based $\textbf{S}$equential neural decoder for $\textbf{P}$olar codes (CRISP). We design a principled curriculum, guided by information-theoretic insights, to train CRISP and show that it outperforms the successive-cancellation (SC) decoder and attains near-optimal reliability performance on the Polar(32,16) and Polar(64,22) codes. The choice of the proposed curriculum is critical in achieving the accuracy gains of CRISP, as we show by comparing against other curricula. More notably, CRISP can be readily extended to Polarization-Adjusted-Convolutional (PAC) codes, where existing SC decoders are significantly less reliable. To the best of our knowledge, CRISP constructs the first data-driven decoder for PAC codes and attains near-optimal performance on the PAC(32,16) code.
abstract_zh: Polar 码是广泛使用的最先进的可靠通信代码，最近已被纳入第五代无线标准 (5G)。然而，在短块长度范围内高效且可靠的极性解码器的设计仍有空间。受数据驱动通道解码器最近成功的启发，我们引入了一种新颖的基于$\textbf{C}$ur$\textbf{RI}$culum的$\textbf{S}$顺序神经解码器，用于$\textbf{P}$olar码（CRISP）。我们设计了一个原则性的课程，以信息论见解为指导，来训练 CRISP，并表明它优于连续取消 (SC) 解码器，并在 Polar(32,16) 和 Polar(64,22) 码上获得接近最佳的可靠性性能。正如我们通过与其他课程的比较所表明的那样，所提议的课程的选择对于实现 CRISP 的准确性增益至关重要。更值得注意的是，CRISP 可以轻松扩展到偏振调整卷积 (PAC) 代码，而现有 SC 解码器的可靠性明显较差。据我们所知，CRISP 构建了第一个 PAC 代码的数据驱动解码器，并在 PAC(32,16) 代码上获得了近乎最佳的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于课程学习的序列神经解码器CRISP，用于改进极化码的解码性能。
AI.motivation: 极化码已被广泛应用于5G通信，但在短块长度情况下，其解码器的效率和可靠性仍有待提高。
AI.method: 本文设计了一种基于信息论指导的课程学习策略，用于训练CRISP解码器，以提高其解码性能。
AI.result: CRISP在Polar(32,16)和Polar(64,22)码上表现出优于逐次消去解码器的性能，并且可以扩展到PAC码，实现接近最优的性能。
AI.conclusion: CRISP是首个针对PAC码的数据驱动解码器，其课程学习策略在提升解码性能方面具有关键作用。

authors_text: Ningyu Zhang,Lei Li,Xiang Chen,Xiaozhuan Liang,Shumin Deng,Huajun Chen
title: Multimodal Analogical Reasoning over Knowledge Graphs
title_zh: 知识图谱的多模态类比推理
comment: Accepted by ICLR 2023. The project website is https://zjunlp.github.io/project/MKG_Analogy/introduction.html
comments: Accepted by ICLR 2023. The project website is https://zjunlp.github.io/project/MKG_Analogy/introduction.html
abstract: Analogical reasoning is fundamental to human cognition and holds an important place in various fields. However, previous studies mainly focus on single-modal analogical reasoning and ignore taking advantage of structure knowledge. Notably, the research in cognitive psychology has demonstrated that information from multimodal sources always brings more powerful cognitive transfer than single modality sources. To this end, we introduce the new task of multimodal analogical reasoning over knowledge graphs, which requires multimodal reasoning ability with the help of background knowledge. Specifically, we construct a Multimodal Analogical Reasoning dataSet (MARS) and a multimodal knowledge graph MarKG. We evaluate with multimodal knowledge graph embedding and pre-trained Transformer baselines, illustrating the potential challenges of the proposed task. We further propose a novel model-agnostic Multimodal analogical reasoning framework with Transformer (MarT) motivated by the structure mapping theory, which can obtain better performance. Code and datasets are available in https://github.com/zjunlp/MKG_Analogy.
summary: Analogical reasoning is fundamental to human cognition and holds an important place in various fields. However, previous studies mainly focus on single-modal analogical reasoning and ignore taking advantage of structure knowledge. Notably, the research in cognitive psychology has demonstrated that information from multimodal sources always brings more powerful cognitive transfer than single modality sources. To this end, we introduce the new task of multimodal analogical reasoning over knowledge graphs, which requires multimodal reasoning ability with the help of background knowledge. Specifically, we construct a Multimodal Analogical Reasoning dataSet (MARS) and a multimodal knowledge graph MarKG. We evaluate with multimodal knowledge graph embedding and pre-trained Transformer baselines, illustrating the potential challenges of the proposed task. We further propose a novel model-agnostic Multimodal analogical reasoning framework with Transformer (MarT) motivated by the structure mapping theory, which can obtain better performance. Code and datasets are available in https://github.com/zjunlp/MKG_Analogy.
abstract_zh: 类比推理是人类认知的基础，在各个领域都占有重要地位。然而，以往的研究主要集中于单模态类比推理，而忽视了结构知识的利用。值得注意的是，认知心理学的研究表明，多模态来源的信息总是比单一模态来源的信息带来更强大的认知迁移。为此，我们引入了知识图谱多模态类比推理的新任务，这需要借助背景知识进行多模态推理能力。具体来说，我们构建了多模态类比推理数据集（MARS）和多模态知识图 MarKG。我们使用多模态知识图嵌入和预先训练的 Transformer 基线进行评估，说明了所提出的任务的潜在挑战。我们进一步在结构映射理论的推动下提出了一种新颖的模型不可知的多模态类比推理框架 Transformer (MarT)，它可以获得更好的性能。代码和数据集可在 https://github.com/zjunlp/MKG_Analogy 中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出多模态类比推理任务，利用知识图谱中的结构信息和多模态数据进行类比推理，并构建了相应的数据集和模型框架。
AI.motivation: 现有研究主要关注单模态类比推理，忽视了多模态信息与结构知识的结合，而认知心理学表明多模态信息能提升认知迁移能力。
AI.method: 构建多模态类比推理数据集MARS和知识图谱MarKG，提出基于结构映射理论的模型无关框架MarT，结合Transformer进行多模态推理。
AI.result: 实验表明MarT在多模态知识图谱嵌入和预训练Transformer基线模型上取得更优性能，验证了任务的挑战性和方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 多模态类比推理框架MarT能够有效整合多模态信息与结构知识，为知识图谱中的复杂推理任务提供了新思路。

search_text: Multimodal Analogical Reasoning over Knowledge Graphs 知识图谱的多模态类比推理 Analogical reasoning is fundamental to human cognition and holds an important place in various fields. However, previous studies mainly focus on single-modal analogical reasoning and ignore taking advantage of structure knowledge. Notably, the research in cognitive psychology has demonstrated that information from multimodal sources always brings more powerful cognitive transfer than single modality sources. To this end, we introduce the new task of multimodal analogical reasoning over knowledge graphs, which requires multimodal reasoning ability with the help of background knowledge. Specifically, we construct a Multimodal Analogical Reasoning dataSet (MARS) and a multimodal knowledge graph MarKG. We evaluate with multimodal knowledge graph embedding and pre-trained Transformer baselines, illustrating the potential challenges of the proposed task. We further propose a novel model-agnostic Multimodal analogical reasoning framework with Transformer (MarT) motivated by the structure mapping theory, which can obtain better performance. Code and datasets are available in https://github.com/zjunlp/MKG_Analogy. 类比推理是人类认知的基础，在各个领域都占有重要地位。然而，以往的研究主要集中于单模态类比推理，而忽视了结构知识的利用。值得注意的是，认知心理学的研究表明，多模态来源的信息总是比单一模态来源的信息带来更强大的认知迁移。为此，我们引入了知识图谱多模态类比推理的新任务，这需要借助背景知识进行多模态推理能力。具体来说，我们构建了多模态类比推理数据集（MARS）和多模态知识图 MarKG。我们使用多模态知识图嵌入和预先训练的 Transformer 基线进行评估，说明了所提出的任务的潜在挑战。我们进一步在结构映射理论的推动下提出了一种新颖的模型不可知的多模态类比推理框架 Transformer (MarT)，它可以获得更好的性能。代码和数据集可在 https://github.com/zjunlp/MKG_Analogy 中找到。 本文提出多模态类比推理任务，利用知识图谱中的结构信息和多模态数据进行类比推理，并构建了相应的数据集和模型框架。 Ningyu Zhang,Lei Li,Xiang Chen,Xiaozhuan Liang,Shumin Deng,Huajun Chen cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.MM

authors_text: Xin Xie,Zhoubo Li,Xiaohan Wang,Zekun Xi,Ningyu Zhang
title: LambdaKG: A Library for Pre-trained Language Model-Based Knowledge Graph Embeddings
title_zh: LambdaKG：基于预训练语言模型的知识图嵌入库
comment: AACL 2023 System Demonstrations, the project website is https://zjunlp.github.io/project/promptkg/
comments: AACL 2023 System Demonstrations, the project website is https://zjunlp.github.io/project/promptkg/
abstract: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) often have two characteristics: heterogeneous graph structure and text-rich entity/relation information. Text-based KG embeddings can represent entities by encoding descriptions with pre-trained language models, but no open-sourced library is specifically designed for KGs with PLMs at present. In this paper, we present LambdaKG, a library for KGE that equips with many pre-trained language models (e.g., BERT, BART, T5, GPT-3), and supports various tasks (e.g., knowledge graph completion, question answering, recommendation, and knowledge probing). LambdaKG is publicly open-sourced at https://github.com/zjunlp/PromptKG/tree/main/lambdaKG, with a demo video at http://deepke.zjukg.cn/lambdakg.mp4 and long-term maintenance.
summary: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) often have two characteristics: heterogeneous graph structure and text-rich entity/relation information. Text-based KG embeddings can represent entities by encoding descriptions with pre-trained language models, but no open-sourced library is specifically designed for KGs with PLMs at present. In this paper, we present LambdaKG, a library for KGE that equips with many pre-trained language models (e.g., BERT, BART, T5, GPT-3), and supports various tasks (e.g., knowledge graph completion, question answering, recommendation, and knowledge probing). LambdaKG is publicly open-sourced at https://github.com/zjunlp/PromptKG/tree/main/lambdaKG, with a demo video at http://deepke.zjukg.cn/lambdakg.mp4 and long-term maintenance.
abstract_zh: 知识图（KG）通常具有两个特征：异构图结构和丰富的文本实体/关系信息。基于文本的知识图谱嵌入可以通过使用预先训练的语言模型对描述进行编码来表示实体，但目前还没有专门针对具有 PLM 的知识图谱设计的开源库。在本文中，我们提出了 LambdaKG，一个 KGE 库，它配备了许多预训练的语言模型（例如 BERT、BART、T5、GPT-3），并支持各种任务（例如知识图补全、问答、推荐和知识探测）。 LambdaKG 公开开源于 https://github.com/zjunlp/PromptKG/tree/main/lambdaKG，演示视频位于 http://deepke.zjukg.cn/lambdakg.mp4 并长期维护。

AI.tldr: 开发基于预训练语言模型的知识图谱嵌入库，支持多种下游任务。
AI.motivation: 现有工具缺乏针对KG与PLM结合的开源库，限制了研究和应用。
AI.method: 构建集成BERT、BART等预训练模型的LambdaKG库，支持知识图谱补全、问答等任务。
AI.result: 开源LambdaKG库，提供演示视频及长期维护，支持多种任务。
AI.conclusion: 提出首个针对KG与PLM结合的开源库，推动相关研究与应用。

title: Federated Representation Learning via Maximal Coding Rate Reduction
title_zh: 通过最大编码率降低进行联合表示学习
abstract: We propose a federated methodology to learn low-dimensional representations from a dataset that is distributed among several clients. In particular, we move away from the commonly-used cross-entropy loss in federated learning, and seek to learn shared low-dimensional representations of the data in a decentralized manner via the principle of maximal coding rate reduction (MCR2). Our proposed method, which we refer to as FLOW, utilizes MCR2 as the objective of choice, hence resulting in representations that are both between-class discriminative and within-class compressible. We theoretically show that our distributed algorithm achieves a first-order stationary point. Moreover, we demonstrate, via numerical experiments, the utility of the learned low-dimensional representations.
summary: We propose a federated methodology to learn low-dimensional representations from a dataset that is distributed among several clients. In particular, we move away from the commonly-used cross-entropy loss in federated learning, and seek to learn shared low-dimensional representations of the data in a decentralized manner via the principle of maximal coding rate reduction (MCR2). Our proposed method, which we refer to as FLOW, utilizes MCR2 as the objective of choice, hence resulting in representations that are both between-class discriminative and within-class compressible. We theoretically show that our distributed algorithm achieves a first-order stationary point. Moreover, we demonstrate, via numerical experiments, the utility of the learned low-dimensional representations.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种联合方法，从分布在多个客户端的数据集中学习低维表示。特别是，我们摆脱了联邦学习中常用的交叉熵损失，并寻求通过最大编码率降低（MCR2）原理以分散的方式学习数据的共享低维表示。我们提出的方法（我们称之为 FLOW）利用 MCR2 作为选择目标，因此产生的表示既具有类间区分性又具有类内可压缩性。我们从理论上证明我们的分布式算法实现了一阶驻点。此外，我们通过数值实验证明了所学习的低维表示的实用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种联邦学习方法FLOW，通过最大编码率减少原则学习分布式数据的低维表示。
AI.motivation: 传统联邦学习依赖交叉熵损失，难以在去中心化环境中有效学习共享的低维表示，需探索新目标函数以提升表示能力。
AI.method: 采用最大编码率减少（MCR2）作为目标函数，设计FLOW算法，在保证类间判别性的同时实现类内可压缩性。
AI.result: 理论证明算法可收敛至一阶平稳点，数值实验验证了低维表示的有效性。
AI.conclusion: FLOW方法在联邦表示学习中能有效平衡类间区分与类内压缩，为分布式学习提供新思路。

title: An Ensemble of Convolutional Neural Networks to Detect Foliar Diseases in Apple Plants
title_zh: 用于检测苹果植物叶部疾病的卷积神经网络集合
abstract: Apple diseases, if not diagnosed early, can lead to massive resource loss and pose a serious threat to humans and animals who consume the infected apples. Hence, it is critical to diagnose these diseases early in order to manage plant health and minimize the risks associated with them. However, the conventional approach of monitoring plant diseases entails manual scouting and analyzing the features, texture, color, and shape of the plant leaves, resulting in delayed diagnosis and misjudgments. Our work proposes an ensembled system of Xception, InceptionResNet, and MobileNet architectures to detect 5 different types of apple plant diseases. The model has been trained on the publicly available Plant Pathology 2021 dataset and can classify multiple diseases in a given plant leaf. The system has achieved outstanding results in multi-class and multi-label classification and can be used in a real-time setting to monitor large apple plantations to aid the farmers manage their yields effectively.
summary: Apple diseases, if not diagnosed early, can lead to massive resource loss and pose a serious threat to humans and animals who consume the infected apples. Hence, it is critical to diagnose these diseases early in order to manage plant health and minimize the risks associated with them. However, the conventional approach of monitoring plant diseases entails manual scouting and analyzing the features, texture, color, and shape of the plant leaves, resulting in delayed diagnosis and misjudgments. Our work proposes an ensembled system of Xception, InceptionResNet, and MobileNet architectures to detect 5 different types of apple plant diseases. The model has been trained on the publicly available Plant Pathology 2021 dataset and can classify multiple diseases in a given plant leaf. The system has achieved outstanding results in multi-class and multi-label classification and can be used in a real-time setting to monitor large apple plantations to aid the farmers manage their yields effectively.
abstract_zh: 苹果病害如果不及早诊断，可能会导致大量资源损失，并对食用受感染苹果的人类和动物构成严重威胁。因此，尽早诊断这些疾病对于管理植物健康并最大程度地降低与之相关的风险至关重要。然而，传统的植物病害监测方法需要人工寻找和分析植物叶片的特征、质地、颜色和形状，导致诊断延迟和误判。我们的工作提出了一个 Xception、InceptionResNet 和 MobileNet 架构的集成系统来检测 5 种不同类型的苹果植物病害。该模型已在公开的 Plant Pathology 2021 数据集上进行了训练，可以对给定植物叶子中的多种疾病进行分类。该系统在多类别、多标签分类方面取得了突出的效果，可用于实时监控大型苹果种植园，帮助农民有效管理产量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合Xception、InceptionResNet和MobileNet的卷积神经网络集成系统，用于实时检测苹果植株叶部疾病的分类方法。
AI.motivation: 传统人工检测苹果病害存在效率低、易误判的问题，而早期诊断对减少资源损失和保障食品安全至关重要。
AI.method: 采用Xception、InceptionResNet和MobileNet三种架构构建集成模型，基于Plant Pathology 2021数据集进行多类别和多标签分类训练。
AI.result: 模型在多类别和多标签分类任务中表现优异，可有效应用于大规模苹果种植园的实时监测场景。
AI.conclusion: 该集成系统能为农民提供高效的病害诊断工具，有助于提升果园管理效率并降低经济损失。

tldr: 本文提出一种结合Xception、InceptionResNet和MobileNet的卷积神经网络集成系统，用于实时检测苹果植株叶部疾病的分类方法。
motivation: 传统人工检测苹果病害存在效率低、易误判的问题，而早期诊断对减少资源损失和保障食品安全至关重要。
method: 采用Xception、InceptionResNet和MobileNet三种架构构建集成模型，基于Plant Pathology 2021数据集进行多类别和多标签分类训练。
result: 模型在多类别和多标签分类任务中表现优异，可有效应用于大规模苹果种植园的实时监测场景。
conclusion: 该集成系统能为农民提供高效的病害诊断工具，有助于提升果园管理效率并降低经济损失。
search_text: An Ensemble of Convolutional Neural Networks to Detect Foliar Diseases in Apple Plants 用于检测苹果植物叶部疾病的卷积神经网络集合 Apple diseases, if not diagnosed early, can lead to massive resource loss and pose a serious threat to humans and animals who consume the infected apples. Hence, it is critical to diagnose these diseases early in order to manage plant health and minimize the risks associated with them. However, the conventional approach of monitoring plant diseases entails manual scouting and analyzing the features, texture, color, and shape of the plant leaves, resulting in delayed diagnosis and misjudgments. Our work proposes an ensembled system of Xception, InceptionResNet, and MobileNet architectures to detect 5 different types of apple plant diseases. The model has been trained on the publicly available Plant Pathology 2021 dataset and can classify multiple diseases in a given plant leaf. The system has achieved outstanding results in multi-class and multi-label classification and can be used in a real-time setting to monitor large apple plantations to aid the farmers manage their yields effectively. 苹果病害如果不及早诊断，可能会导致大量资源损失，并对食用受感染苹果的人类和动物构成严重威胁。因此，尽早诊断这些疾病对于管理植物健康并最大程度地降低与之相关的风险至关重要。然而，传统的植物病害监测方法需要人工寻找和分析植物叶片的特征、质地、颜色和形状，导致诊断延迟和误判。我们的工作提出了一个 Xception、InceptionResNet 和 MobileNet 架构的集成系统来检测 5 种不同类型的苹果植物病害。该模型已在公开的 Plant Pathology 2021 数据集上进行了训练，可以对给定植物叶子中的多种疾病进行分类。该系统在多类别、多标签分类方面取得了突出的效果，可用于实时监控大型苹果种植园，帮助农民有效管理产量。 本文提出一种结合Xception、InceptionResNet和MobileNet的卷积神经网络集成系统，用于实时检测苹果植株叶部疾病的分类方法。 Kush Vora,Dishant Padalia cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Timilehin B. Aderinola,Tee Connie,Thian Song Ong,Andrew Beng Jin Teoh,Michael Kah Ong Goh
title: Gait-based Age Group Classification with Adaptive Graph Neural Network
title_zh: 使用自适应图神经网络进行基于步态的年龄组分类
abstract: Deep learning techniques have recently been utilized for model-free age-associated gait feature extraction. However, acquiring model-free gait demands accurate pre-processing such as background subtraction, which is non-trivial in unconstrained environments. On the other hand, model-based gait can be obtained without background subtraction and is less affected by covariates. For model-based gait-based age group classification problems, present works rely solely on handcrafted features, where feature extraction is tedious and requires domain expertise. This paper proposes a deep learning approach to extract age-associated features from model-based gait for age group classification. Specifically, we first develop an unconstrained gait dataset called Multimedia University Gait Age and Gender dataset (MMU GAG). Next, the body joint coordinates are determined via pose estimation algorithms and represented as compact gait graphs via a novel part aggregation scheme. Then, a Part-AdaptIve Residual Graph Convolutional Neural Network (PairGCN) is designed for age-associated feature learning. Experiments suggest that PairGCN features are far more informative than handcrafted features, yielding up to 99% accuracy for classifying subjects as a child, adult, or senior in the MMU GAG dataset. These results suggest the feasibility of deploying Artificial Intelligence-enabled solutions for access control, surveillance, and law enforcement in unconstrained environments.
summary: Deep learning techniques have recently been utilized for model-free age-associated gait feature extraction. However, acquiring model-free gait demands accurate pre-processing such as background subtraction, which is non-trivial in unconstrained environments. On the other hand, model-based gait can be obtained without background subtraction and is less affected by covariates. For model-based gait-based age group classification problems, present works rely solely on handcrafted features, where feature extraction is tedious and requires domain expertise. This paper proposes a deep learning approach to extract age-associated features from model-based gait for age group classification. Specifically, we first develop an unconstrained gait dataset called Multimedia University Gait Age and Gender dataset (MMU GAG). Next, the body joint coordinates are determined via pose estimation algorithms and represented as compact gait graphs via a novel part aggregation scheme. Then, a Part-AdaptIve Residual Graph Convolutional Neural Network (PairGCN) is designed for age-associated feature learning. Experiments suggest that PairGCN features are far more informative than handcrafted features, yielding up to 99% accuracy for classifying subjects as a child, adult, or senior in the MMU GAG dataset. These results suggest the feasibility of deploying Artificial Intelligence-enabled solutions for access control, surveillance, and law enforcement in unconstrained environments.
abstract_zh: 深度学习技术最近已被用于无模型的年龄相关步态特征提取。然而，获取无模型步态需要精确的预处理，例如背景扣除，这在不受约束的环境中并非易事。另一方面，基于模型的步态无需背景扣除即可获得，并且受协变量的影响较小。对于基于模型的步态年龄组分类问题，目前的工作仅依赖于手工制作的特征，其中特征提取非常繁琐并且需要领域专业知识。本文提出了一种深度学习方法，从基于模型的步态中提取与年龄相关的特征，以进行年龄组分类。具体来说，我们首先开发一个名为多媒体大学步态年龄和性别数据集（MMU GAG）的无约束步态数据集。接下来，通过姿势估计算法确定身体关节坐标，并通过新颖的部分聚合方案将其表示为紧凑的步态图。然后，设计了部分自适应残差图卷积神经网络（PairGCN），用于与年龄相关的特征学习。实验表明，PairGCN 特征比手工特征提供的信息要丰富得多，在 MMU GAG 数据集中将受试者分类为儿童、成人或老年人时，准确率高达 99%。这些结果表明在不受约束的环境中部署支持人工智能的访问控制、监视和执法解决方案的可行性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自适应图神经网络的步态年龄分组分类方法，通过构建新型数据集和设计PairGCN模型实现高精度分类。
AI.motivation: 现有步态年龄分类方法依赖手工特征提取且需复杂预处理，难以适应非受限环境。模型基步态虽可避免背景分割，但缺乏有效深度学习特征提取方法。
AI.method: 构建MMU GAG数据集，通过姿态估计获取关节点坐标并采用部件聚合方案生成紧凑步态图，设计Part-Adaptive残差图卷积神经网络（PairGCN）进行年龄相关特征学习。
AI.result: 在MMU GAG数据集上实现儿童/成人/老年三类分类最高99%准确率，证明PairGCN特征显著优于手工特征。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了AI驱动的步态年龄分类在非受限环境下的可行性，可应用于安防、门禁等实际场景。

title: Deep Intrinsically Motivated Exploration in Continuous Control
title_zh: 持续控制中的深层内在动机探索
abstract: In continuous control, exploration is often performed through undirected strategies in which parameters of the networks or selected actions are perturbed by random noise. Although the deep setting of undirected exploration has been shown to improve the performance of on-policy methods, they introduce an excessive computational complexity and are known to fail in the off-policy setting. The intrinsically motivated exploration is an effective alternative to the undirected strategies, but they are usually studied for discrete action domains. In this paper, we investigate how intrinsic motivation can effectively be combined with deep reinforcement learning in the control of continuous systems to obtain a directed exploratory behavior. We adapt the existing theories on animal motivational systems into the reinforcement learning paradigm and introduce a novel and scalable directed exploration strategy. The introduced approach, motivated by the maximization of the value function's error, can benefit from a collected set of experiences by extracting useful information and unify the intrinsic exploration motivations in the literature under a single exploration objective. An extensive set of empirical studies demonstrate that our framework extends to larger and more diverse state spaces, dramatically improves the baselines, and outperforms the undirected strategies significantly.
summary: In continuous control, exploration is often performed through undirected strategies in which parameters of the networks or selected actions are perturbed by random noise. Although the deep setting of undirected exploration has been shown to improve the performance of on-policy methods, they introduce an excessive computational complexity and are known to fail in the off-policy setting. The intrinsically motivated exploration is an effective alternative to the undirected strategies, but they are usually studied for discrete action domains. In this paper, we investigate how intrinsic motivation can effectively be combined with deep reinforcement learning in the control of continuous systems to obtain a directed exploratory behavior. We adapt the existing theories on animal motivational systems into the reinforcement learning paradigm and introduce a novel and scalable directed exploration strategy. The introduced approach, motivated by the maximization of the value function's error, can benefit from a collected set of experiences by extracting useful information and unify the intrinsic exploration motivations in the literature under a single exploration objective. An extensive set of empirical studies demonstrate that our framework extends to larger and more diverse state spaces, dramatically improves the baselines, and outperforms the undirected strategies significantly.
abstract_zh: 在连续控制中，探索通常是通过无向策略进行的，其中网络参数或选定的动作受到随机噪声的干扰。尽管无向探索的深层设置已被证明可以提高同策略方法的性能，但它们引入了过多的计算复杂性，并且已知在离策略设置中会失败。内在动机探索是无向策略的有效替代方案，但它们通常针对离散的行动领域进行研究。在本文中，我们研究了如何将内在动机有效地与深度强化学习相结合来控制连续系统以获得定向探索行为。我们将现有的动物激励系统理论应用到强化学习范式中，并引入一种新颖且可扩展的定向探索策略。所引入的方法以价值函数误差最大化为动机，可以通过提取有用信息从收集的经验中受益，并将文献中的内在探索动机统一在单一探索目标下。大量的实证研究表明，我们的框架扩展到更大、更多样化的状态空间，显着改善了基线，并且显着优于无向策略。

AI.tldr: 研究如何将内在动机有效结合到深度强化学习中，以在连续控制系统中实现定向探索。
AI.motivation: 传统无方向探索策略在连续控制中存在计算复杂度高、离线策略失效等问题，而现有内在动机方法多局限于离散动作域。
AI.method: 借鉴动物动机系统理论，设计基于价值函数误差最大化的定向探索策略，整合经验数据并统一内在探索目标。
AI.result: 框架适用于更大状态空间，显著优于传统无方向策略及基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 提出的新方法有效解决了连续控制中的探索难题，为复杂环境下的强化学习提供新方向。

An extensive set of empirical studies demonstrate that our framework extends to larger and more diverse state spaces, dramatically improves the baselines, and outperforms the undirected strategies significantly. 在连续控制中，探索通常是通过无向策略进行的，其中网络参数或选定的动作受到随机噪声的干扰。尽管无向探索的深层设置已被证明可以提高同策略方法的性能，但它们引入了过多的计算复杂性，并且已知在离策略设置中会失败。内在动机探索是无向策略的有效替代方案，但它们通常针对离散的行动领域进行研究。在本文中，我们研究了如何将内在动机有效地与深度强化学习相结合来控制连续系统以获得定向探索行为。我们将现有的动物激励系统理论应用到强化学习范式中，并引入一种新颖且可扩展的定向探索策略。所引入的方法以价值函数误差最大化为动机，可以通过提取有用信息从收集的经验中受益，并将文献中的内在探索动机统一在单一探索目标下。大量的实证研究表明，我们的框架扩展到更大、更多样化的状态空间，显着改善了基线，并且显着优于无向策略。 研究如何将内在动机有效结合到深度强化学习中，以在连续控制系统中实现定向探索。 Baturay Saglam,Suleyman S. Kozat cs.LG,cs.AI

title: DeltaBound Attack: Efficient decision-based attack in low queries regime
title_zh: DeltaBound Attack：低查询条件下的高效基于决策的攻击
abstract: Deep neural networks and other machine learning systems, despite being extremely powerful and able to make predictions with high accuracy, are vulnerable to adversarial attacks. We proposed the DeltaBound attack: a novel, powerful attack in the hard-label setting with $\ell_2$ norm bounded perturbations. In this scenario, the attacker has only access to the top-1 predicted label of the model and can be therefore applied to real-world settings such as remote API. This is a complex problem since the attacker has very little information about the model. Consequently, most of the other techniques present in the literature require a massive amount of queries for attacking a single example. Oppositely, this work mainly focuses on the evaluation of attack's power in the low queries regime $\leq 1000$ queries) with $\ell_2$ norm in the hard-label settings. We find that the DeltaBound attack performs as well and sometimes better than current state-of-the-art attacks while remaining competitive across different kinds of models. Moreover, we evaluate our method against not only deep neural networks, but also non-deep learning models, such as Gradient Boosting Decision Trees and Multinomial Naive Bayes.
summary: Deep neural networks and other machine learning systems, despite being extremely powerful and able to make predictions with high accuracy, are vulnerable to adversarial attacks. We proposed the DeltaBound attack: a novel, powerful attack in the hard-label setting with $\ell_2$ norm bounded perturbations. In this scenario, the attacker has only access to the top-1 predicted label of the model and can be therefore applied to real-world settings such as remote API. This is a complex problem since the attacker has very little information about the model. Consequently, most of the other techniques present in the literature require a massive amount of queries for attacking a single example. Oppositely, this work mainly focuses on the evaluation of attack's power in the low queries regime $\leq 1000$ queries) with $\ell_2$ norm in the hard-label settings. We find that the DeltaBound attack performs as well and sometimes better than current state-of-the-art attacks while remaining competitive across different kinds of models. Moreover, we evaluate our method against not only deep neural networks, but also non-deep learning models, such as Gradient Boosting Decision Trees and Multinomial Naive Bayes.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络和其他机器学习系统尽管非常强大并且能够做出高精度预测，但很容易受到对抗性攻击。我们提出了 DeltaBound 攻击：一种在硬标签设置中新颖、强大的攻击，具有 $\ell_2$ 范数有界扰动。在这种情况下，攻击者只能访问模型的 top-1 预测标签，因此可以应用于真实世界的设置，例如远程 API。这是一个复杂的问题，因为攻击者对模型的信息很少。因此，文献中存在的大多数其他技术需要大量查询来攻击单个示例。相反，这项工作主要关注在硬标签设置中使用 $\ell_2$ 范数的低查询机制 $\leq 1000$ 查询中评估攻击的威力。我们发现 DeltaBound 攻击的性能与当前最先进的攻击一样好，有时甚至更好，同时在不同类型的模型中保持竞争力。此外，我们不仅针对深度神经网络，还针对非深度学习模型（例如梯度提升决策树和多项式朴素贝叶斯）来评估我们的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DeltaBound的新型对抗攻击方法，在低查询次数（≤1000次）和硬标签设置下实现高效决策型攻击。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗攻击方法在硬标签场景下需大量查询，难以应用于实际场景（如远程API）。本文旨在开发一种在有限查询次数下仍具高攻击效率的方法。
AI.method: DeltaBound通过ℓ2范数约束的扰动，在仅获取模型输出的top-1标签信息时，利用梯度估计和边界约束优化攻击向量。
AI.result: DeltaBound在多种深度学习模型（如CNN、RNN）及非深度模型（如GBDT、朴素贝叶斯）上均表现优异，攻击成功率与现有最先进的方法相当或更优。
AI.conclusion: DeltaBound在低查询次数下实现了高效的硬标签攻击，验证了其在实际场景中的可行性和广泛适用性。

title: NeuroEvo: A Cloud-based Platform for Automated Design and Training of Neural Networks using Evolutionary and Particle Swarm Algorithms
title_zh: NeuroEvo：基于云的平台，使用进化和粒子群算法自动设计和训练神经网络
abstract: Evolutionary algorithms (EAs) provide unique advantages for optimizing neural networks in complex search spaces. This paper introduces a new web platform, NeuroEvo (neuroevo.io), that allows users to interactively design and train neural network classifiers using evolutionary and particle swarm algorithms. The classification problem and training data are provided by the user and, upon completion of the training process, the best classifier is made available to download and implement in Python, Java, and JavaScript. NeuroEvo is a cloud-based application that leverages GPU parallelization to improve the speed with which the independent evolutionary steps, such as mutation, crossover, and fitness evaluation, are executed across the population. This paper outlines the training algorithms and opportunities for users to specify design decisions and hyperparameter settings. The algorithms described in this paper are also made available as a Python package, neuroevo (PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/neuroevo/).
summary: Evolutionary algorithms (EAs) provide unique advantages for optimizing neural networks in complex search spaces. This paper introduces a new web platform, NeuroEvo (neuroevo.io), that allows users to interactively design and train neural network classifiers using evolutionary and particle swarm algorithms. The classification problem and training data are provided by the user and, upon completion of the training process, the best classifier is made available to download and implement in Python, Java, and JavaScript. NeuroEvo is a cloud-based application that leverages GPU parallelization to improve the speed with which the independent evolutionary steps, such as mutation, crossover, and fitness evaluation, are executed across the population. The algorithms described in this paper are also made available as a Python package, neuroevo (PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/neuroevo/).
abstract_zh: 进化算法 (EA) 为优化复杂搜索空间中的神经网络提供了独特的优势。本文介绍了一个新的网络平台 NeuroEvo (neuroevo.io)，它允许用户使用进化和粒子群算法交互式设计和训练神经网络分类器。分类问题和训练数据由用户提供，训练过程完成后，可以下载最佳分类器并用 Python、Java 和 JavaScript 实现。 NeuroEvo 是一个基于云的应用程序，它利用 GPU 并行化来提高在群体中执行独立进化步骤（例如突变、交叉和适应度评估）的速度。本文概述了训练算法以及用户指定设计决策和超参数设置的机会。本文中描述的算法也可以作为 Python 包 Neuroevo 提供（PyPI：https://pypi.org/project/neuroevo/）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于云的平台NeuroEvo，通过进化算法和粒子群算法实现神经网络分类器的自动化设计与训练。
AI.motivation: 传统神经网络设计依赖人工调参，面临复杂搜索空间优化困难、训练效率低等问题，需借助进化算法等自动化方法提升效率。
AI.method: 基于云平台实现GPU并行加速，结合进化算法（EA）和粒子群优化（PSO）进行神经网络结构优化，用户可自定义超参数并下载训练结果，同时提供Python包支持本地调用。
AI.result: 开发了交互式Web平台NeuroEvo（neuroevo.io）及Python库neuroevo，支持多语言部署，通过GPU加速显著提升训练效率。
AI.conclusion: NeuroEvo为用户提供了一种高效、易用的神经网络自动化设计工具，通过云原生架构和群体智能算法优化了模型训练流程。

title: Swift Markov Logic for Probabilistic Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs
title_zh: 用于知识图概率推理的快速马尔可夫逻辑
comment: Under consideration in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP)
comments: Under consideration in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP)
abstract: We provide a framework for probabilistic reasoning in Vadalog-based Knowledge Graphs (KGs), satisfying the requirements of ontological reasoning: full recursion, powerful existential quantification, expression of inductive definitions. Vadalog is a Knowledge Representation and Reasoning (KRR) language based on Warded Datalog+/-, a logical core language of existential rules, with a good balance between computational complexity and expressive power. Handling uncertainty is essential for reasoning with KGs. Yet Vadalog and Warded Datalog+/- are not covered by the existing probabilistic logic programming and statistical relational learning approaches for several reasons, including insufficient support for recursion with existential quantification, and the impossibility to express inductive definitions. In this work, we introduce Soft Vadalog, a probabilistic extension to Vadalog, satisfying these desiderata. A Soft Vadalog program induces what we call a Probabilistic Knowledge Graph (PKG), which consists of a probability distribution on a network of chase instances, structures obtained by grounding the rules over a database using the chase procedure. We exploit PKGs for probabilistic marginal inference. We discuss the theory and present MCMC-chase, a Monte Carlo method to use Soft Vadalog in practice. We apply our framework to solve data management and industrial problems, and experimentally evaluate it in the Vadalog system. Under consideration in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP).

We exploit PKGs for probabilistic marginal inference. We discuss the theory and present MCMC-chase, a Monte Carlo method to use Soft Vadalog in practice. We apply our framework to solve data management and industrial problems, and experimentally evaluate it in the Vadalog system. Under consideration in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP).
abstract_zh: 我们提供了基于 Vadalog 的知识图谱（KG）中的概率推理框架，满足本体推理的要求：完全递归、强大的存在量化、归纳定义的表达。 Vadalog 是一种基于 Warded Datalog+/- 的知识表示和推理（KRR）语言，是存在规则的逻辑核心语言，在计算复杂度和表达能力之间具有良好的平衡。处理不确定性对于 KG 推理至关重要。然而，由于多种原因，现有的概率逻辑编程和统计关系学习方法并未涵盖 Vadalog 和 Warded Datalog+/-，其中包括对存在量化递归的支持不足，以及无法表达归纳定义。在这项工作中，我们引入了 Soft Vadalog，它是 Vadalog 的概率扩展，满足了这些需求。 Soft Vadalog 程序产生了我们所说的概率知识图（PKG），它由追踪实例网络上的概率分布组成，这些结构是通过使用追踪程序将规则建立在数据库上而获得的。我们利用 PKG 进行概率边际推理。我们讨论了该理论并提出了 MCMC-chase，这是一种在实践中使用 Soft Vadalog 的蒙特卡罗方法。我们应用我们的框架来解决数据管理和工业问题，并在 Vadalog 系统中对其进行实验评估。正在逻辑编程理论与实践（TPLP）中考虑。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Soft Vadalog框架，用于在基于Vadalog的知识图谱中进行概率推理，解决现有方法在处理递归、存在量化和归纳定义方面的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有概率逻辑编程和统计关系学习方法无法满足Vadalog对递归与存在量化结合及归纳定义的表达需求，而知识图谱推理需要处理不确定性。
AI.method: 引入Soft Vadalog作为Vadalog的概率扩展，构建概率知识图谱(PKG)并利用MCMC-chase方法进行概率边缘推理，结合工业场景验证有效性。
AI.result: 框架成功应用于数据管理及工业问题，实验表明其在Vadalog系统中可有效处理不确定性推理任务。
AI.conclusion: Soft Vadalog通过概率扩展解决了Vadalog的推理局限，为知识图谱提供了兼顾表达力与计算效率的概率推理方案。

search_text: Swift Markov Logic for Probabilistic Reasoning on Knowledge Graphs 用于知识图概率推理的快速马尔可夫逻辑 We provide a framework for probabilistic reasoning in Vadalog-based Knowledge Graphs (KGs), satisfying the requirements of ontological reasoning: full recursion, powerful existential quantification, expression of inductive definitions. Vadalog is a Knowledge Representation and Reasoning (KRR) language based on Warded Datalog+/-, a logical core language of existential rules, with a good balance between computational complexity and expressive power. Handling uncertainty is essential for reasoning with KGs. Yet Vadalog and Warded Datalog+/- are not covered by the existing probabilistic logic programming and statistical relational learning approaches for several reasons, including insufficient support for recursion with existential quantification, and the impossibility to express inductive definitions. In this work, we introduce Soft Vadalog, a probabilistic extension to Vadalog, satisfying these desiderata. A Soft Vadalog program induces what we call a Probabilistic Knowledge Graph (PKG), which consists of a probability distribution on a network of chase instances, structures obtained by grounding the rules over a database using the chase procedure. We exploit PKGs for probabilistic marginal inference. We discuss the theory and present MCMC-chase, a Monte Carlo method to use Soft Vadalog in practice. We apply our framework to solve data management and industrial problems, and experimentally evaluate it in the Vadalog system. Under consideration in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP). 我们提供了基于 Vadalog 的知识图谱（KG）中的概率推理框架，满足本体推理的要求：完全递归、强大的存在量化、归纳定义的表达。 Vadalog 是一种基于 Warded Datalog+/- 的知识表示和推理（KRR）语言，是存在规则的逻辑核心语言，在计算复杂度和表达能力之间具有良好的平衡。处理不确定性对于 KG 推理至关重要。然而，由于多种原因，现有的概率逻辑编程和统计关系学习方法并未涵盖 Vadalog 和 Warded Datalog+/-，其中包括对存在量化递归的支持不足，以及无法表达归纳定义。在这项工作中，我们引入了 Soft Vadalog，它是 Vadalog 的概率扩展，满足了这些需求。 Soft Vadalog 程序产生了我们所说的概率知识图（PKG），它由追踪实例网络上的概率分布组成，这些结构是通过使用追踪程序将规则建立在数据库上而获得的。我们利用 PKG 进行概率边际推理。我们讨论了该理论并提出了 MCMC-chase，这是一种在实践中使用 Soft Vadalog 的蒙特卡罗方法。我们应用我们的框架来解决数据管理和工业问题，并在 Vadalog 系统中对其进行实验评估。正在逻辑编程理论与实践（TPLP）中考虑。 本文提出Soft Vadalog框架，用于在基于Vadalog的知识图谱中进行概率推理，解决现有方法在处理递归、存在量化和归纳定义方面的不足。 Luigi Bellomarini,Eleonora Laurenza,Emanuel Sallinger,Evgeny Sherkhonov cs.AI

authors_text: Shuhei Mandokoro,Natsuki Oka,Akane Matsushima,Chie Fukada,Yuko Yoshimura,Koji Kawahara,Kazuaki Tanaka
title: Construction and Evaluation of a Self-Attention Model for Semantic Understanding of Sentence-Final Particles
title_zh: 句末助词语义理解的自注意力模型的构建和评估
comment: 4 pages, 1 figure. Published in the Program and Abstract Booklet of Workshop on Constructive Approaches to Co-Creative Communication in Joint Conference on Language Evolution, 2022
comments: 4 pages, 1 figure. Published in the Program and Abstract Booklet of Workshop on Constructive Approaches to Co-Creative Communication in Joint Conference on Language Evolution, 2022
abstract: Sentence-final particles serve an essential role in spoken Japanese because they express the speaker's mental attitudes toward a proposition and/or an interlocutor. They are acquired at early ages and occur very frequently in everyday conversation. However, there has been little proposal for a computational model of acquiring sentence-final particles. This paper proposes Subjective BERT, a self-attention model that takes various subjective senses in addition to language and images as input and learns the relationship between words and subjective senses. An evaluation experiment revealed that the model understands the usage of "yo", which expresses the speaker's intention to communicate new information, and that of "ne", which denotes the speaker's desire to confirm that some information is shared.
summary: Sentence-final particles serve an essential role in spoken Japanese because they express the speaker's mental attitudes toward a proposition and/or an interlocutor. They are acquired at early ages and occur very frequently in everyday conversation. However, there has been little proposal for a computational model of acquiring sentence-final particles. This paper proposes Subjective BERT, a self-attention model that takes various subjective senses in addition to language and images as input and learns the relationship between words and subjective senses. An evaluation experiment revealed that the model understands the usage of "yo", which expresses the speaker's intention to communicate new information, and that of "ne", which denotes the speaker's desire to confirm that some information is shared.
abstract_zh: 句末助词在日语口语中起着至关重要的作用，因为它们表达了说话者对命题和/或对话者的心理态度。它们是在很小的时候就习得的，并且在日常对话中经常出现。然而，关于获取句子最终粒子的计算模型几乎没有提出。本文提出了主观 BERT，一种自注意力模型，将语言和图像之外的各种主观感觉作为输入，学习单词和主观感觉之间的关系。评估实验表明，该模型理解“yo”的用法，“yo”表达了说话者传达新信息的意图，以及“ne”的用法，“ne”表示说话者希望确认某些信息被共享。

AI.tldr: 构建并评估一个自注意力模型，用于理解日语句子末尾语气词的语义。
AI.motivation: 日语句子末尾语气词能表达说话者的态度，但缺乏计算模型对其进行研究。
AI.method: 提出Subjective BERT模型，结合语言、图像和主观感受作为输入，学习词语与主观感受的关系。
AI.result: 实验表明模型能有效理解'yo'（传达新信息）和'ne'（确认信息共享）的用法。
AI.conclusion: 该模型为理解语气词提供了有效方法，未来可扩展至其他语言或场景。

title: Det-SLAM: A semantic visual SLAM for highly dynamic scenes using Detectron2
title_zh: Det-SLAM：使用 Detectron2 用于高动态场景的语义视觉 SLAM
abstract: According to experts, Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is an intrinsic part of autonomous robotic systems. Several SLAM systems with impressive performance have been invented and used during the last several decades. However, there are still unresolved issues, such as how to deal with moving objects in dynamic situations. Classic SLAM systems depend on the assumption of a static environment, which becomes unworkable in highly dynamic situations. Several methods have been presented to tackle this issue in recent years, but each has its limitations. This research combines the visual SLAM systems ORB-SLAM3 and Detectron2 to present the Det-SLAM system, which employs depth information and semantic segmentation to identify and eradicate dynamic spots to accomplish semantic SLAM for dynamic situations. Evaluation of public TUM datasets indicates that Det-SLAM is more resilient than previous dynamic SLAM systems and can lower the estimated error of camera posture in dynamic indoor scenarios.
summary: According to experts, Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is an intrinsic part of autonomous robotic systems. Several SLAM systems with impressive performance have been invented and used during the last several decades. However, there are still unresolved issues, such as how to deal with moving objects in dynamic situations. Classic SLAM systems depend on the assumption of a static environment, which becomes unworkable in highly dynamic situations. Several methods have been presented to tackle this issue in recent years, but each has its limitations. This research combines the visual SLAM systems ORB-SLAM3 and Detectron2 to present the Det-SLAM system, which employs depth information and semantic segmentation to identify and eradicate dynamic spots to accomplish semantic SLAM for dynamic situations. Evaluation of public TUM datasets indicates that Det-SLAM is more resilient than previous dynamic SLAM systems and can lower the estimated error of camera posture in dynamic indoor scenarios.
abstract_zh: 专家表示，同步定位与建图（SLAM）是自主机器人系统的固有组成部分。在过去的几十年里，已经发明并使用了多种性能令人印象深刻的 SLAM 系统。然而，仍然存在一些未解决的问题，例如如何处理动态情况下的移动物体。经典的 SLAM 系统依赖于静态环境的假设，这在高度动态的情况下变得不可行。近年来，已经提出了几种方法来解决这个问题，但每种方法都有其局限性。本研究结合视觉SLAM系统ORB-SLAM3和Detectron2提出了Det-SLAM系统，该系统利用深度信息和语义分割来识别和消除动态点，以实现动态情况下的语义SLAM。对公共 TUM 数据集的评估表明，Det-SLAM 比以前的动态 SLAM 系统更具弹性，可以降低动态室内场景中摄像机姿态的估计误差。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Det-SLAM系统，结合视觉SLAM与语义分割技术，解决高度动态场景下的定位与建图问题。
AI.motivation: 传统SLAM系统依赖静态环境假设，在动态场景中表现不佳，现有动态SLAM方法存在局限性，需更鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 融合ORB-SLAM3视觉SLAM框架与Detectron2语义分割模型，通过深度信息和语义分割识别动态区域并消除其影响。
AI.result: 在TUM数据集测试中，Det-SLAM相较现有动态SLAM系统展现出更强鲁棒性，显著降低动态室内场景的相机姿态估计误差。
AI.conclusion: Det-SLAM通过语义信息有效处理动态场景，为高动态环境下的SLAM问题提供了更优解决方案。

Classic SLAM systems depend on the assumption of a static environment, which becomes unworkable in highly dynamic situations. Several methods have been presented to tackle this issue in recent years, but each has its limitations. This research combines the visual SLAM systems ORB-SLAM3 and Detectron2 to present the Det-SLAM system, which employs depth information and semantic segmentation to identify and eradicate dynamic spots to accomplish semantic SLAM for dynamic situations. Evaluation of public TUM datasets indicates that Det-SLAM is more resilient than previous dynamic SLAM systems and can lower the estimated error of camera posture in dynamic indoor scenarios. 专家表示，同步定位与建图（SLAM）是自主机器人系统的固有组成部分。在过去的几十年里，已经发明并使用了多种性能令人印象深刻的 SLAM 系统。然而，仍然存在一些未解决的问题，例如如何处理动态情况下的移动物体。经典的 SLAM 系统依赖于静态环境的假设，这在高度动态的情况下变得不可行。近年来，已经提出了几种方法来解决这个问题，但每种方法都有其局限性。本研究结合视觉SLAM系统ORB-SLAM3和Detectron2提出了Det-SLAM系统，该系统利用深度信息和语义分割来识别和消除动态点，以实现动态情况下的语义SLAM。对公共 TUM 数据集的评估表明，Det-SLAM 比以前的动态 SLAM 系统更具弹性，可以降低动态室内场景中摄像机姿态的估计误差。 本文提出Det-SLAM系统，结合视觉SLAM与语义分割技术，解决高度动态场景下的定位与建图问题。 Ali Eslamian,Mohammad R. Ahmadzadeh cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Mesay Samuel,Lars Schmidt-Thieme,DP Sharma,Abiot Sinamo,Abey Bruck
title: Offline Handwritten Amharic Character Recognition Using Few-shot Learning
title_zh: 使用少样本学习进行离线手写阿姆哈拉语字符识别
abstract: Few-shot learning is an important, but challenging problem of machine learning aimed at learning from only fewer labeled training examples. It has become an active area of research due to deep learning requiring huge amounts of labeled dataset, which is not feasible in the real world. Learning from a few examples is also an important attempt towards learning like humans. Few-shot learning has proven a very good promise in different areas of machine learning applications, particularly in image classification. As it is a recent technique, most researchers focus on understanding and solving the issues related to its concept by focusing only on common image datasets like Mini-ImageNet and Omniglot. Few-shot learning also opens an opportunity to address low resource languages like Amharic. In this study, offline handwritten Amharic character recognition using few-shot learning is addressed. Particularly, prototypical networks, the popular and simpler type of few-shot learning, is implemented as a baseline. Using the opportunities explored in the nature of Amharic alphabet having row-wise and column-wise similarities, a novel way of augmenting the training episodes is proposed. The experimental results show that the proposed method outperformed the baseline method. This study has implemented few-shot learning for Amharic characters for the first time. More importantly, the findings of the study open new ways of examining the influence of training episodes in few-shot learning, which is one of the important issues that needs exploration. The datasets used for this study are collected from native Amharic language writers using an Android App developed as a part of this study.
