authors_text: Jeremy A. Collins,Patrick Grady,Charles C. Kemp
title: Force/Torque Sensing for Soft Grippers using an External Camera
title_zh: 使用外部摄像头对软夹具进行力/扭矩传感
comment: Accepted for presentation at 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: Accepted for presentation at 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Robotic manipulation can benefit from wrist-mounted force/torque (F/T) sensors, but conventional F/T sensors can be expensive, difficult to install, and damaged by high loads. We present Visual Force/Torque Sensing (VFTS), a method that visually estimates the 6-axis F/T measurement that would be reported by a conventional F/T sensor. In contrast to approaches that sense loads using internal cameras placed behind soft exterior surfaces, our approach uses an external camera with a fisheye lens that observes a soft gripper. VFTS includes a deep learning model that takes a single RGB image as input and outputs a 6-axis F/T estimate. We trained the model with sensor data collected while teleoperating a robot (Stretch RE1 from Hello Robot Inc.) to perform manipulation tasks. VFTS outperformed F/T estimates based on motor currents, generalized to a novel home environment, and supported three autonomous tasks relevant to healthcare: grasping a blanket, pulling a blanket over a manikin, and cleaning a manikin's limbs. VFTS also performed well with a manually operated pneumatic gripper. Overall, our results suggest that an external camera observing a soft gripper can perform useful visual force/torque sensing for a variety of manipulation tasks.
summary: Robotic manipulation can benefit from wrist-mounted force/torque (F/T) sensors, but conventional F/T sensors can be expensive, difficult to install, and damaged by high loads. We present Visual Force/Torque Sensing (VFTS), a method that visually estimates the 6-axis F/T measurement that would be reported by a conventional F/T sensor. In contrast to approaches that sense loads using internal cameras placed behind soft exterior surfaces, our approach uses an external camera with a fisheye lens that observes a soft gripper. VFTS includes a deep learning model that takes a single RGB image as input and outputs a 6-axis F/T estimate. We trained the model with sensor data collected while teleoperating a robot (Stretch RE1 from Hello Robot Inc.) to perform manipulation tasks. VFTS outperformed F/T estimates based on motor currents, generalized to a novel home environment, and supported three autonomous tasks relevant to healthcare: grasping a blanket, pulling a blanket over a manikin, and cleaning a manikin's limbs. VFTS also performed well with a manually operated pneumatic gripper. Overall, our results suggest that an external camera observing a soft gripper can perform useful visual force/torque sensing for a variety of manipulation tasks.
abstract_zh: 机器人操纵可以受益于安装在手腕上的力/扭矩 (F/T) 传感器，但传统的 F/T 传感器可能价格昂贵、难以安装，并且会因高负载而损坏。我们提出了视觉力/扭矩传感 (VFTS)，这是一种视觉估计传统 F/T 传感器报告的 6 轴 F/T 测量值的方法。与使用放置在柔软外表面后面的内部摄像头感测负载的方法相比，我们的方法使用带有鱼眼镜头的外部摄像头来观察柔软的夹具。 VFTS 包括一个深度学习模型，该模型将单个 RGB 图像作为输入并输出 6 轴 F/T 估计。我们使用远程操作机器人（Hello Robot Inc. 的 Stretch RE1）执行操作任务时收集的传感器数据来训练模型。 VFTS 的性能优于基于电机电流的 F/T 估计，可推广到新的家庭环境，并支持与医疗保健相关的三项自主任务：抓住毯子、将毯子拉到人体模型上以及清洁人体模型的四肢。 VFTS 在使用手动气动夹具时也表现良好。总的来说，我们的结果表明，观察软夹具的外部摄像头可以对各种操纵任务执行有用的视觉力/扭矩传感。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于外部摄像头的视觉力/扭矩传感方法（VFTS），用于软夹爪的6轴力/扭矩估计。
AI.motivation: 传统力/扭矩传感器成本高、安装困难且易损，需寻找更鲁棒且经济的替代方案。
AI.method: 使用鱼眼镜头外部摄像头观测软夹爪，结合深度学习模型，通过单帧RGB图像输出6轴力/扭矩估计，模型在远程操作机器人数据集上训练。
AI.result: VFTS优于基于电机电流的估计，在新环境泛化良好，并成功完成抓取毯子、覆盖人偶及清洁肢体等医疗任务，且适用于手动气动夹爪。
AI.conclusion: 外部摄像头观测软夹爪的视觉力/扭矩传感方法在多种操作任务中有效，可作为传统传感器的可行替代方案。

title: Differentially Private Optimization on Large Model at Small Cost
title_zh: 小成本大型模型的差分隐私优化
abstract: Differentially private (DP) optimization is the standard paradigm to learn large neural networks that are accurate and privacy-preserving. The computational cost for DP deep learning, however, is notoriously heavy due to the per-sample gradient clipping. Existing DP implementations are 2-1000X more costly in time and space complexity than the standard (non-private) training. In this work, we develop a novel Book-Keeping (BK) technique that implements existing DP optimizers (thus achieving the same accuracy), with a substantial improvement on the computational cost. Specifically, BK enables DP training on large models and high dimensional data to be roughly as fast and memory-saving as the standard training, whereas previous DP algorithms can be inefficient or incapable of training due to memory error. The computational advantage of BK is supported by the complexity analysis as well as extensive experiments on vision and language tasks. Our implementation achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) accuracy with very small extra cost: on GPT2 and at almost the same memory cost (<1% overhead), BK has 1.03X the time complexity of the standard training (0.83X training speed in practice), and 0.61X the time complexity of the most efficient DP implementation (1.36X training speed in practice). We open-source the codebase for the BK algorithm at the FastDP library (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy).

Specifically, BK enables DP training on large models and high dimensional data to be roughly as fast and memory-saving as the standard training, whereas previous DP algorithms can be inefficient or incapable of training due to memory error. The computational advantage of BK is supported by the complexity analysis as well as extensive experiments on vision and language tasks. Our implementation achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) accuracy with very small extra cost: on GPT2 and at almost the same memory cost (<1% overhead), BK has 1.03X the time complexity of the standard training (0.83X training speed in practice), and 0.61X the time complexity of the most efficient DP implementation (1.36X training speed in practice). We open-source the codebase for the BK algorithm at the FastDP library (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy).
abstract_zh: 差分隐私（DP）优化是学习准确且保护隐私的大型神经网络的标准范例。然而，由于每个样本的梯度裁剪，DP 深度学习的计算成本非常高。现有的 DP 实施在时间和空间复杂度上的成本比标准（非私人）培训高 2-1000 倍。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种新颖的簿记（BK）技术，该技术实现了现有的 DP 优化器（从而达到相同的精度），并显着改善了计算成本。具体来说，BK 使得大型模型和高维数据上的 DP 训练大致与标准训练一样快且节省内存，而之前的 DP 算法可能会因内存错误而效率低下或无法进行训练。 BK 的计算优势得到了复杂性分析以及视觉和语言任务的广泛实验的支持。我们的实现以非常小的额外成本实现了最先进的 (SOTA) 精度：在 GPT2 上，在几乎相同的内存成本（<1% 开销）下，BK 的时间复杂度是标准训练的 1.03 倍（实践中的训练速度为 0.83 倍），是最高效 DP 实现的时间复杂度的 0.61 倍（实践中的训练速度为 1.36 倍）。我们在 FastDP 库 (https://github.com/awslabs/fast- Differential-privacy) 中开源了 BK 算法的代码库。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Book-Keeping（BK）的高效差分隐私优化技术，使大规模模型在保持隐私性的同时显著降低计算成本。
AI.motivation: 现有差分隐私（DP）深度学习方法因逐样本梯度裁剪导致计算复杂度比标准训练高2-1000倍，存在效率低下和内存溢出问题。
AI.method: 开发BK技术，通过优化梯度处理流程，在不牺牲精度的前提下减少计算和内存开销，实现与标准训练相近的效率。
AI.result: 在GPT2等任务中，BK方法仅增加<1%内存开销，时间复杂度达标准训练的1.03倍（实际训练速度提升至0.83倍），比最高效DP实现快1.36倍。
AI.conclusion: BK技术通过算法改进有效解决了DP训练的高成本问题，为大规模模型隐私保护提供了实用化方案。

search_text: Differentially Private Optimization on Large Model at Small Cost 小成本大型模型的差分隐私优化 Differentially private (DP) optimization is the standard paradigm to learn large neural networks that are accurate and privacy-preserving. The computational cost for DP deep learning, however, is notoriously heavy due to the per-sample gradient clipping. Existing DP implementations are 2-1000X more costly in time and space complexity than the standard (non-private) training. In this work, we develop a novel Book-Keeping (BK) technique that implements existing DP optimizers (thus achieving the same accuracy), with a substantial improvement on the computational cost. Specifically, BK enables DP training on large models and high dimensional data to be roughly as fast and memory-saving as the standard training, whereas previous DP algorithms can be inefficient or incapable of training due to memory error. The computational advantage of BK is supported by the complexity analysis as well as extensive experiments on vision and language tasks. Our implementation achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) accuracy with very small extra cost: on GPT2 and at almost the same memory cost (<1% overhead), BK has 1.03X the time complexity of the standard training (0.83X training speed in practice), and 0.61X the time complexity of the most efficient DP implementation (1.36X training speed in practice). We open-source the codebase for the BK algorithm at the FastDP library (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy). 差分隐私（DP）优化是学习准确且保护隐私的大型神经网络的标准范例。然而，由于每个样本的梯度裁剪，DP 深度学习的计算成本非常高。现有的 DP 实施在时间和空间复杂度上的成本比标准（非私人）培训高 2-1000 倍。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种新颖的簿记（BK）技术，该技术实现了现有的 DP 优化器（从而达到相同的精度），并显着改善了计算成本。具体来说，BK 使得大型模型和高维数据上的 DP 训练大致与标准训练一样快且节省内存，而之前的 DP 算法可能会因内存错误而效率低下或无法进行训练。 BK 的计算优势得到了复杂性分析以及视觉和语言任务的广泛实验的支持。我们的实现以非常小的额外成本实现了最先进的 (SOTA) 精度：在 GPT2 上，在几乎相同的内存成本（<1% 开销）下，BK 的时间复杂度是标准训练的 1.03 倍（实践中的训练速度为 0.83 倍），是最高效 DP 实现的时间复杂度的 0.61 倍（实践中的训练速度为 1.36 倍）。我们在 FastDP 库 (https://github.com/awslabs/fast- Differential-privacy) 中开源了 BK 算法的代码库。 本文提出一种名为Book-Keeping（BK）的高效差分隐私优化技术，使大规模模型在保持隐私性的同时显著降低计算成本。 Zhiqi Bu,Yu-Xiang Wang,Sheng Zha,George Karypis cs.LG,cs.CL,cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Koresh Khateri,Karthik Soma,Mahdi Pourgholi,Mohsen Montazeri,Lorenzo Sabattini,Giovanni Beltrame
title: A Complete Set of Connectivity-aware Local Topology Manipulation Operations for Robot Swarms
title_zh: 一套完整的机器人群连接感知本地拓扑操作操作
abstract: The topology of a robotic swarm affects the convergence speed of consensus and the mobility of the robots. In this paper, we prove the existence of a complete set of local topology manipulation operations that allow the transformation of a swarm topology. The set is complete in the sense that any other possible set of manipulation operations can be performed by a sequence of operations from our set. The operations are local as they depend only on the first and second hop neighbors' information to transform any initial spanning tree of the network's graph to any other connected tree with the same number of nodes. The flexibility provided by our method is similar to global methods that require full knowledge of the swarm network. We prove the existence of a sequence of transformations for any tree-to-tree transformation, and derive sequences of operations to form a line or star from any initial spanning tree. Our work provides a theoretical and practical framework for topological control of a swarm, establishing global properties using only local information.
summary: The topology of a robotic swarm affects the convergence speed of consensus and the mobility of the robots. In this paper, we prove the existence of a complete set of local topology manipulation operations that allow the transformation of a swarm topology. The set is complete in the sense that any other possible set of manipulation operations can be performed by a sequence of operations from our set. The operations are local as they depend only on the first and second hop neighbors' information to transform any initial spanning tree of the network's graph to any other connected tree with the same number of nodes. The flexibility provided by our method is similar to global methods that require full knowledge of the swarm network. We prove the existence of a sequence of transformations for any tree-to-tree transformation, and derive sequences of operations to form a line or star from any initial spanning tree. Our work provides a theoretical and practical framework for topological control of a swarm, establishing global properties using only local information.
abstract_zh: 机器人群的拓扑结构影响共识的收敛速度和机器人的移动性。在本文中，我们证明了一组完整的局部拓扑操作操作的存在，这些操作允许集群拓扑的转换。该集合是完整的，因为任何其他可能的操纵操作集合都可以通过我们集合中的一系列操作来执行。这些操作是本地的，因为它们仅依赖于第一跳和第二跳邻居的信息来将网络图的任何初始生成树转换为具有相同节点数的任何其他连接树。我们的方法提供的灵活性类似于需要充分了解群体网络的全局方法。我们证明任何树到树变换的变换序列的存在性，并导出从任何初始生成树形成线或星形的操作序列。我们的工作为群的拓扑控制提供了理论和实践框架，仅使用局部信息建立全局属性。

AI.tldr: 提出一套基于局部信息的完整连接感知拓扑操作集，用于机器人集群的拓扑转换。
AI.motivation: 机器人集群的拓扑结构影响共识收敛速度和移动性，传统方法依赖全局信息，而本文旨在提供仅需局部信息的灵活拓扑控制方法。
AI.method: 通过证明局部操作集的完整性，利用一跳和两跳邻居信息，实现从任意初始生成树到目标拓扑的转换。
AI.result: 证明了任意树到树转换的序列存在性，并推导出生成线型或星型结构的操作序列。
AI.conclusion: 建立了仅依赖局部信息的机器人集群拓扑控制理论框架，实现了全局拓扑属性的灵活调整。

title: Differentially Private Bias-Term Fine-tuning of Foundation Models
title_zh: 基础模型的差分私人偏置项微调
abstract: We study the problem of differentially private (DP) fine-tuning of large pre-trained models -- a recent privacy-preserving approach suitable for solving downstream tasks with sensitive data. Existing work has demonstrated that high accuracy is possible under strong privacy constraint, yet requires significant computational overhead or modifications to the network architecture. We propose differentially private bias-term fine-tuning (DP-BiTFiT), which matches the state-of-the-art accuracy for DP algorithms and the efficiency of the standard BiTFiT. DP-BiTFiT is model agnostic (not modifying the network architecture), parameter efficient (only training about 0.1% of the parameters), and computation efficient (almost removing the overhead caused by DP, in both the time and space complexity). On a wide range of tasks, DP-BiTFiT is 2~30X faster and uses 2~8X less memory than DP full fine-tuning, even faster than the standard full fine-tuning. This amazing efficiency enables us to conduct DP fine-tuning on language and vision tasks with long-sequence texts and high-resolution images, which were computationally difficult using existing methods. We open-source our code at FastDP (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy).
summary: We study the problem of differentially private (DP) fine-tuning of large pre-trained models -- a recent privacy-preserving approach suitable for solving downstream tasks with sensitive data. Existing work has demonstrated that high accuracy is possible under strong privacy constraint, yet requires significant computational overhead or modifications to the network architecture. We propose differentially private bias-term fine-tuning (DP-BiTFiT), which matches the state-of-the-art accuracy for DP algorithms and the efficiency of the standard BiTFiT. DP-BiTFiT is model agnostic (not modifying the network architecture), parameter efficient (only training about 0.1% of the parameters), and computation efficient (almost removing the overhead caused by DP, in both the time and space complexity). On a wide range of tasks, DP-BiTFiT is 2~30X faster and uses 2~8X less memory than DP full fine-tuning, even faster than the standard full fine-tuning. This amazing efficiency enables us to conduct DP fine-tuning on language and vision tasks with long-sequence texts and high-resolution images, which were computationally difficult using existing methods. We open-source our code at FastDP (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy).
abstract_zh: 我们研究大型预训练模型的差分隐私（DP）微调问题——一种最新的隐私保护方法，适用于解决敏感数据的下游任务。现有的工作已经证明，在严格的隐私约束下，高精度是可能的，但需要大量的计算开销或对网络架构的修改。我们提出了差分隐私偏差项微调（DP-BiTFiT），它与 DP 算法的最先进精度和标准 BiTFiT 的效率相匹配。 DP-BiTFiT 与模型无关（不修改网络架构）、参数高效（仅训练约 0.1% 的参数）和计算高效（在时间和空间复杂度上几乎消除了 DP 造成的开销）。在广泛的任务上，DP-BiTFiT 比 DP 全微调快 2~30 倍，使用的内存少 2~8 倍，甚至比标准全微调更快。这种惊人的效率使我们能够对具有长序列文本和高分辨率图像的语言和视觉任务进行 DP 微调，而使用现有方法在计算上是困难的。我们在 FastDP (https://github.com/awslabs/fast- Differential-privacy) 上开源了我们的代码。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种差分隐私（DP）偏置项微调方法（DP-BiTFiT），用于在保护隐私的同时高效微调大型预训练模型。
AI.motivation: 现有DP微调方法在强隐私约束下虽能保持高精度，但计算开销大或需修改网络结构，限制了其应用。
AI.method: DP-BiTFiT通过仅训练约0.1%的参数（偏置项）实现模型无关、参数高效和计算高效的DP微调，几乎消除DP带来的额外开销。
AI.result: 在多种任务中，DP-BiTFiT比DP全量微调快2~30倍，内存消耗减少2~8倍，且支持处理长文本和高分辨率图像。
AI.conclusion: DP-BiTFiT在隐私保护、精度和效率间取得平衡，为复杂任务的DP微调提供了可行方案。

tldr: 本文提出了一种差分隐私（DP）偏置项微调方法（DP-BiTFiT），用于在保护隐私的同时高效微调大型预训练模型。
motivation: 现有DP微调方法在强隐私约束下虽能保持高精度，但计算开销大或需修改网络结构，限制了其应用。
method: DP-BiTFiT通过仅训练约0.1%的参数（偏置项）实现模型无关、参数高效和计算高效的DP微调，几乎消除DP带来的额外开销。
result: 在多种任务中，DP-BiTFiT比DP全量微调快2~30倍，内存消耗减少2~8倍，且支持处理长文本和高分辨率图像。
conclusion: DP-BiTFiT在隐私保护、精度和效率间取得平衡，为复杂任务的DP微调提供了可行方案。
search_text: Differentially Private Bias-Term Fine-tuning of Foundation Models 基础模型的差分私人偏置项微调 We study the problem of differentially private (DP) fine-tuning of large pre-trained models -- a recent privacy-preserving approach suitable for solving downstream tasks with sensitive data. Existing work has demonstrated that high accuracy is possible under strong privacy constraint, yet requires significant computational overhead or modifications to the network architecture. We propose differentially private bias-term fine-tuning (DP-BiTFiT), which matches the state-of-the-art accuracy for DP algorithms and the efficiency of the standard BiTFiT. DP-BiTFiT is model agnostic (not modifying the network architecture), parameter efficient (only training about 0.1% of the parameters), and computation efficient (almost removing the overhead caused by DP, in both the time and space complexity). On a wide range of tasks, DP-BiTFiT is 2~30X faster and uses 2~8X less memory than DP full fine-tuning, even faster than the standard full fine-tuning. This amazing efficiency enables us to conduct DP fine-tuning on language and vision tasks with long-sequence texts and high-resolution images, which were computationally difficult using existing methods. We open-source our code at FastDP (https://github.com/awslabs/fast-differential-privacy). 我们研究大型预训练模型的差分隐私（DP）微调问题——一种最新的隐私保护方法，适用于解决敏感数据的下游任务。现有的工作已经证明，在严格的隐私约束下，高精度是可能的，但需要大量的计算开销或对网络架构的修改。我们提出了差分隐私偏差项微调（DP-BiTFiT），它与 DP 算法的最先进精度和标准 BiTFiT 的效率相匹配。 DP-BiTFiT 与模型无关（不修改网络架构）、参数高效（仅训练约 0.1% 的参数）和计算高效（在时间和空间复杂度上几乎消除了 DP 造成的开销）。在广泛的任务上，DP-BiTFiT 比 DP 全微调快 2~30 倍，使用的内存少 2~8 倍，甚至比标准全微调更快。这种惊人的效率使我们能够对具有长序列文本和高分辨率图像的语言和视觉任务进行 DP 微调，而使用现有方法在计算上是困难的。我们在 FastDP (https://github.com/awslabs/fast- Differential-privacy) 上开源了我们的代码。 本文提出了一种差分隐私（DP）偏置项微调方法（DP-BiTFiT），用于在保护隐私的同时高效微调大型预训练模型。 Zhiqi Bu,Yu-Xiang Wang,Sheng Zha,George Karypis cs.LG,cs.CL,cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Yecheng Jason Ma,Shagun Sodhani,Dinesh Jayaraman,Osbert Bastani,Vikash Kumar,Amy Zhang
title: VIP: Towards Universal Visual Reward and Representation via Value-Implicit Pre-Training
title_zh: VIP：通过价值隐式预训练实现通用视觉奖励和表示
abstract: Reward and representation learning are two long-standing challenges for learning an expanding set of robot manipulation skills from sensory observations. Given the inherent cost and scarcity of in-domain, task-specific robot data, learning from large, diverse, offline human videos has emerged as a promising path towards acquiring a generally useful visual representation for control; however, how these human videos can be used for general-purpose reward learning remains an open question. We introduce $\textbf{V}$alue-$\textbf{I}$mplicit $\textbf{P}$re-training (VIP), a self-supervised pre-trained visual representation capable of generating dense and smooth reward functions for unseen robotic tasks. VIP casts representation learning from human videos as an offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning problem and derives a self-supervised dual goal-conditioned value-function objective that does not depend on actions, enabling pre-training on unlabeled human videos. Theoretically, VIP can be understood as a novel implicit time contrastive objective that generates a temporally smooth embedding, enabling the value function to be implicitly defined via the embedding distance, which can then be used to construct the reward for any goal-image specified downstream task. Trained on large-scale Ego4D human videos and without any fine-tuning on in-domain, task-specific data, VIP's frozen representation can provide dense visual reward for an extensive set of simulated and $\textbf{real-robot}$ tasks, enabling diverse reward-based visual control methods and significantly outperforming all prior pre-trained representations. Notably, VIP can enable simple, $\textbf{few-shot}$ offline RL on a suite of real-world robot tasks with as few as 20 trajectories.
summary: Reward and representation learning are two long-standing challenges for learning an expanding set of robot manipulation skills from sensory observations. Given the inherent cost and scarcity of in-domain, task-specific robot data, learning from large, diverse, offline human videos has emerged as a promising path towards acquiring a generally useful visual representation for control; however, how these human videos can be used for general-purpose reward learning remains an open question. We introduce $\textbf{V}$alue-$\textbf{I}$mplicit $\textbf{P}$re-training (VIP), a self-supervised pre-trained visual representation capable of generating dense and smooth reward functions for unseen robotic tasks. VIP casts representation learning from human videos as an offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning problem and derives a self-supervised dual goal-conditioned value-function objective that does not depend on actions, enabling pre-training on unlabeled human videos. Theoretically, VIP can be understood as a novel implicit time contrastive objective that generates a temporally smooth embedding, enabling the value function to be implicitly defined via the embedding distance, which can then be used to construct the reward for any goal-image specified downstream task. Trained on large-scale Ego4D human videos and without any fine-tuning on in-domain, task-specific data, VIP's frozen representation can provide dense visual reward for an extensive set of simulated and $\textbf{real-robot}$ tasks, enabling diverse reward-based visual control methods and significantly outperforming all prior pre-trained representations. Notably, VIP can enable simple, $\textbf{few-shot}$ offline RL on a suite of real-world robot tasks with as few as 20 trajectories.
abstract_zh: 奖励和表征学习是通过感官观察学习一系列不断扩展的机器人操作技能的两个长期挑战。考虑到域内特定任务机器人数据的固有成本和稀缺性，从大型、多样化、离线人类视频中学习已成为获取普遍有用的控制视觉表示的有前途的途径；然而，如何将这些人类视频用于通用奖励学习仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。我们引入了$\textbf{V}$alue-$\textbf{I}$mplicit $\textbf{P}$再训练（VIP），这是一种自我监督的预训练视觉表示，能够为看不见的机器人任务生成密集且平滑的奖励函数。 VIP 将人类视频的表示学习视为离线目标条件强化学习问题，并导出不依赖于动作的自监督双重目标条件价值函数目标，从而能够对未标记的人类视频进行预训练。从理论上讲，VIP可以理解为一种新颖的隐式时间对比目标，它生成时间平滑的嵌入，使得价值函数能够通过嵌入距离隐式定义，然后可以用于构建任何目标图像指定的下游任务的奖励。 VIP 的冻结表示经过大规模 Ego4D 人类视频的训练，无需对域内特定任务数据进行任何微调，可以为大量模拟和 $\textbf{real-robot}$ 任务提供密集的视觉奖励，从而实现多种基于奖励的视觉控制方法，并显着优于所有先前的预训练表示。值得注意的是，VIP 可以在一套现实世界的机器人任务上启用简单的、$\textbf{few-shot}$ 离线强化学习，轨迹少至 20 个。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为VIP的自监督预训练方法，通过分析人类视频生成通用视觉奖励函数和表示，以解决机器人操作任务中奖励学习和表示学习的挑战。
AI.motivation: 传统机器人操作技能学习依赖昂贵且稀缺的领域特定数据，而利用大规模离线人类视频进行预训练可获取通用视觉表示，但如何从中学习通用奖励函数仍是未解难题。
AI.method: 将人类视频的表示学习建模为无动作的离线目标条件强化学习问题，设计双目标条件值函数自监督目标，通过隐式时间对比学习生成时序平滑嵌入，使值函数隐式定义为嵌入距离，从而构造任意目标图像任务的奖励。
AI.result: 在Ego4D数据集上预训练的VIP表示无需微调即可为多种模拟和真实机器人任务提供密集视觉奖励，显著优于现有预训练方法，并支持仅需20条轨迹的少样本离线强化学习。
AI.conclusion: VIP通过隐式值函数预训练实现了通用视觉奖励与表示学习，为机器人控制提供了高效且数据高效的解决方案，具有广泛的实际应用潜力。

authors_text: Yash Jakhotiya,Heramb Patil,Jugal Rawlani,Sunil B. Mane
title: Adversarial Attacks on Transformers-Based Malware Detectors
title_zh: 基于 Transformers 的恶意软件检测器的对抗性攻击
comment: Accepted to the 2022 NeurIPS ML Safety Workshop. Code available at https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers
comments: Accepted to the 2022 NeurIPS ML Safety Workshop. Code available at https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers
abstract: Signature-based malware detectors have proven to be insufficient as even a small change in malignant executable code can bypass these signature-based detectors. Many machine learning-based models have been proposed to efficiently detect a wide variety of malware. Many of these models are found to be susceptible to adversarial attacks - attacks that work by generating intentionally designed inputs that can force these models to misclassify. Our work aims to explore vulnerabilities in the current state of the art malware detectors to adversarial attacks. We train a Transformers-based malware detector, carry out adversarial attacks resulting in a misclassification rate of 23.9% and propose defenses that reduce this misclassification rate to half. An implementation of our work can be found at https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers.
summary: Signature-based malware detectors have proven to be insufficient as even a small change in malignant executable code can bypass these signature-based detectors. Many machine learning-based models have been proposed to efficiently detect a wide variety of malware. Many of these models are found to be susceptible to adversarial attacks - attacks that work by generating intentionally designed inputs that can force these models to misclassify. Our work aims to explore vulnerabilities in the current state of the art malware detectors to adversarial attacks. We train a Transformers-based malware detector, carry out adversarial attacks resulting in a misclassification rate of 23.9% and propose defenses that reduce this misclassification rate to half. An implementation of our work can be found at https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers.
abstract_zh: 事实证明，基于签名的恶意软件检测器是不够的，因为即使恶意可执行代码的微小变化也可以绕过这些基于签名的检测器。人们提出了许多基于机器学习的模型来有效地检测各种恶意软件。人们发现，这些模型中的许多都容易受到对抗性攻击——这些攻击通过生成有意设计的输入来起作用，这些输入可以迫使这些模型进行错误分类。我们的工作旨在探索当前最先进的恶意软件检测器中针对对抗性攻击的漏洞。我们训练基于 Transformers 的恶意软件检测器，进行对抗性攻击，导致错误分类率为 23.9%，并提出防御措施，将错误分类率降低一半。我们工作的实现可以在 https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文研究基于Transformer的恶意软件检测器的对抗攻击方法，并提出防御措施。
AI.motivation: 传统签名检测器易被绕过，现有机器学习模型存在对抗攻击漏洞，需提升检测器安全性。
AI.method: 训练基于Transformer的恶意软件检测模型，实施对抗攻击并提出防御策略。
AI.result: 对抗攻击使误分类率达23.9%，防御措施使其降低至11.95%。
AI.conclusion: 对抗攻击有效，防御措施有效，但需进一步研究以增强检测器鲁棒性。

ai_content.conclusion: 对抗攻击有效，防御措施有效，但需进一步研究以增强检测器鲁棒性。

tldr: 本文研究基于Transformer的恶意软件检测器的对抗攻击方法，并提出防御措施。
motivation: 传统签名检测器易被绕过，现有机器学习模型存在对抗攻击漏洞，需提升检测器安全性。
method: 训练基于Transformer的恶意软件检测模型，实施对抗攻击并提出防御策略。

conclusion: 对抗攻击有效，防御措施有效，但需进一步研究以增强检测器鲁棒性。
search_text: Adversarial Attacks on Transformers-Based Malware Detectors 基于 Transformers 的恶意软件检测器的对抗性攻击 Signature-based malware detectors have proven to be insufficient as even a small change in malignant executable code can bypass these signature-based detectors. Many machine learning-based models have been proposed to efficiently detect a wide variety of malware. Many of these models are found to be susceptible to adversarial attacks - attacks that work by generating intentionally designed inputs that can force these models to misclassify. Our work aims to explore vulnerabilities in the current state of the art malware detectors to adversarial attacks. We train a Transformers-based malware detector, carry out adversarial attacks resulting in a misclassification rate of 23.9% and propose defenses that reduce this misclassification rate to half. An implementation of our work can be found at https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers. 事实证明，基于签名的恶意软件检测器是不够的，因为即使恶意可执行代码的微小变化也可以绕过这些基于签名的检测器。人们提出了许多基于机器学习的模型来有效地检测各种恶意软件。人们发现，这些模型中的许多都容易受到对抗性攻击——这些攻击通过生成有意设计的输入来起作用，这些输入可以迫使这些模型进行错误分类。我们的工作旨在探索当前最先进的恶意软件检测器中针对对抗性攻击的漏洞。我们训练基于 Transformers 的恶意软件检测器，进行对抗性攻击，导致错误分类率为 23.9%，并提出防御措施，将错误分类率降低一半。我们工作的实现可以在 https://github.com/yashjakhotiya/Adversarial-Attacks-On-Transformers 找到。 本文研究基于Transformer的恶意软件检测器的对抗攻击方法，并提出防御措施。 Yash Jakhotiya,Heramb Patil,Jugal Rawlani,Sunil B. Mane cs.CR,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Kiarash Jamali,Dari Kimanius,Sjors H. W. Scheres
title: A Graph Neural Network Approach to Automated Model Building in Cryo-EM Maps
title_zh: 用于在 Cryo-EM 图中自动构建模型的图神经网络方法
comment: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations
comments: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations
abstract: Electron cryo-microscopy (cryo-EM) produces three-dimensional (3D) maps of the electrostatic potential of biological macromolecules, including proteins. Along with knowledge about the imaged molecules, cryo-EM maps allow de novo atomic modelling, which is typically done through a laborious manual process. Taking inspiration from recent advances in machine learning applications to protein structure prediction, we propose a graph neural network (GNN) approach for automated model building of proteins in cryo-EM maps. The GNN acts on a graph with nodes assigned to individual amino acids and edges representing the protein chain. Combining information from the voxel-based cryo-EM data, the amino acid sequence data and prior knowledge about protein geometries, the GNN refines the geometry of the protein chain and classifies the amino acids for each of its nodes. Application to 28 test cases shows that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art and approximates manual building for cryo-EM maps with resolutions better than 3.5 Å.
summary: Electron cryo-microscopy (cryo-EM) produces three-dimensional (3D) maps of the electrostatic potential of biological macromolecules, including proteins. Along with knowledge about the imaged molecules, cryo-EM maps allow de novo atomic modelling, which is typically done through a laborious manual process. Taking inspiration from recent advances in machine learning applications to protein structure prediction, we propose a graph neural network (GNN) approach for automated model building of proteins in cryo-EM maps. The GNN acts on a graph with nodes assigned to individual amino acids and edges representing the protein chain. Combining information from the voxel-based cryo-EM data, the amino acid sequence data and prior knowledge about protein geometries, the GNN refines the geometry of the protein chain and classifies the amino acids for each of its nodes. Application to 28 test cases shows that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art and approximates manual building for cryo-EM maps with resolutions better than 3.5 Å.
abstract_zh: 冷冻电子显微镜 (cryo-EM) 可生成生物大分子（包括蛋白质）静电势的三维 (3D) 图。除了有关成像分子的知识之外，冷冻电镜图还可以进行从头原子建模，这通常是通过费力的手动过程完成的。从机器学习应用到蛋白质结构预测的最新进展中汲取灵感，我们提出了一种图神经网络（GNN）方法，用于在冷冻电镜图谱中自动构建蛋白质模型。 GNN 作用于一个图，其中节点分配给各个氨基酸，边代表蛋白质链。 GNN 结合基于体素的冷冻电镜数据、氨基酸序列数据和有关蛋白质几何形状的先验知识，细化了蛋白质链的几何形状，并对每个节点的氨基酸进行了分类。对 28 个测试用例的应用表明，我们的方法优于最先进的方法，并且近似手动构建分辨率优于 3.5 Å 的冷冻电镜图。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于图神经网络（GNN）的自动化方法，用于在低温电子显微镜（cryo-EM）密度图中构建蛋白质原子模型。
AI.motivation: 传统的cryo-EM蛋白质建模依赖耗时的手动过程，而机器学习在蛋白质结构预测中的成功启发了自动化方法的开发。
AI.method: 构建以氨基酸为节点、蛋白质链为边的图结构，结合cryo-EM体素数据、氨基酸序列及蛋白质几何先验知识，通过GNN优化蛋白质几何构型并分类氨基酸。
AI.result: 在28个测试案例中，该方法在分辨率优于3.5Å的cryo-EM图中表现优于现有技术，接近手动建模效果。
AI.conclusion: GNN方法有效实现了cryo-EM密度图的自动化蛋白质建模，有望加速结构生物学研究。
